races.name: Bath & West Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grand Vendetta
runners.spotlight: Cost 140,000euros as 3yo and is half-brother to six winners; runner-up on Chepstow debut (good to soft) in December and he asserted late on when beating a clear second in a novice at Taunton (2m3f, heavy; at 4-5) in early February; sets a good standard under a penalty and he's open to more progress on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Beat a clear second at Taunton and he sets a good standard under a penalty; key player

runners.name: Sogna In Grande
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m hurdle winner from the family of high-class chaser Black Corton; bought for Â£75,000 after winning a British point last spring and he made a good start for new yard when landing a novice hurdle at Warwick (2m5f, soft) in November; showed a good attitude to hold of an odds-on rival in that race and he's respected under a penalty.
runners.comment: Point winner who won on hurdling/stable debut at Warwick (2m5f); respected under penalty

runners.name: Catwalk Girl
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-5 in British points and his win was on good ground; made a promising start over hurdles when third at Ludlow (2m5f, soft) in February but she weakened into a remote fifth at Southwell last month; has something to prove after that and this is a tough assignment.
runners.comment: Promising third on hurdling debut at Ludlow but was tailed off last time
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

runners.name: Amatchmadeinheaven
runners.spotlight: Won a point bumper and followed up in a small field on fast ground at Exeter last March; well held in his final bumper but he returned for new yard with a win on hurdling debut at Exeter (2m, good to soft) last month; needs to improve on that form but he landed a gamble in that race and was strong at the finish; in the mix upped in trip.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who landed gamble on hurdling/stable debut at Exeter; in the mix up in trip

runners.name: Sure To Be Sure
runners.spotlight: Well held in his three bumpers and he's been pulled up at big prices in both hurdle runs (saddle slipped on first occasion); can only be watched.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in his five starts including two runs over hurdles; can only be watched

races.name: Bridles Care Home In Wincanton Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Gold Link
runners.spotlight: 10yo with a record of 3-30; has patchy profile and was tailed off at Warwick last month but he finished a close second at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft) on his penultimate run; looks interesting on that form and first-time cheekpieces could give him a lift.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but was close second at Southwell (2m4f) on penultimate run; new headgear

runners.name: Jukebox King
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who has finished fourth in his three hurdle runs and was only beaten around 4l on handicap debut at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft; 11-2) last month; still has untapped potential and he's a big player if he can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo who was creditable fourth on handicap debut at Warwick (2m5f); interesting

runners.name: Calme Olympien
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 7yo who won three-runner Ffos Las maiden (2m4f, good) on British debut in October; wasn't beaten far in a Sandown handicap next time but he finished a remote fifth at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) in February; dropped another 2lb but he needs to kick on again to make a big impact.
runners.comment: Won a Fontwell maiden in October but no impact back in handicaps since; needs to find more

runners.name: Jena dOudairies
runners.spotlight: Has faded in last four runs but last two have been at around 3m and her win at Chepstow last spring was over an extended 2m3f (good); on reduced mark and she needs a close look on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Has faded in last four runs but this drop back in trip could spark her back into action

runners.name: Wispit Twob
runners.spotlight: Juvenile hurdler who was runner-up in her final start in Ireland in November but she's struggled in both runs for current yard and latest was on handicap debut at Exeter (2m2f, good to soft); still early days but she needs a transformation at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but she's struggled for new yard and needs a transformation at this new trip

runners.name: Matthias
runners.spotlight: Returned from two-year absence with some promising runs during the winter including when fifth of 18 in a maiden here (1m7f, good to soft; tongue tied) in February; now goes handicapping off what looks a fair mark and she has a high-class 2m5f winner on her dam's side; respected at this new trip.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer who has shown promise this winter; needs close look at this new trip

runners.name: Twist Of Fatecatch
runners.spotlight: After his Fontwell maiden (2m3f, good) last March, he ran well in his first three handicaps but he's taken backward steps in last three runs; on dangerous mark but he was tailed off at Newbury in January and needs a major revival after a break.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he needs a major revival after a short break

runners.name: Geezer Rockstar
runners.spotlight: Returned after wind surgery with a near-miss at Chepstow (2m3f, good) last April but he's had only two runs since and has struggled in both of them; hurdles record is now 1-10 and he needs to get back on track after another break.
runners.comment: Has a patchy record and he's struggled in his last two runs; comes with risks attached

races.name: Bridles New Luxury Care Home Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Broomfields Cave
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-4 over fences when landing a C&D handicap (good to soft) on Boxing Day; flopped at Doncaster (pulled up) last time but that was in the Grimthorpe and he could resume his progress on this drop back in grade; needs a close look.
runners.comment: Flopped at Doncaster but that was in the Grimthorpe and he still has potential as a chaser

runners.name: Muskoka
runners.spotlight: Runner-up behind Broomfields Cave over C&D in December but his form has slumped in two subsequent runs (2m7f/3m5f); overall record is now 1-18 and he has something to prove again.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in December but was pulled up at Warwick last time; risks attached

runners.name: Golan Loop
runners.spotlight: Built on his Southwell chase debut second when going one better at Hereford (3m1f, good to soft) in December; had a major blip over C&D next time but he bounced back with a respectable effort on good ground at Hereford (lost his left-hind shoe) last month; still has potential as a chaser and he's in the mix with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed chaser who won over 3m1f in December; in the mix with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Loup De Maulde
runners.spotlight: Completed a double over fences at Taunton (2m7f, soft) in March 2024 but he's returned from 690 days off with two underwhelming runs (hurdles/chase) this spring; looks well handicapped on his old form but he needs to raise his game on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Well treated on old form but he's returned from long absence with two underwhelming runs

runners.name: Law Of Supply
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner (2m7f/3m) in autumn of 2024; lightly raced since but he was disputing third when he fell at the last at Hereford (3m1f, good to soft) in December; returns after another break but conditions should be fine and he's now 1lb lower than for his last win; respected.
runners.comment: Disputing third when he fell at the last at Hereford in December; respected on his return

races.name: Visit Bridles Care Home Wincanton Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Square Du Roule
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Exeter (2m5f, good to soft) in November and he had a near-miss at Taunton (3m, good) the following month; looks interesting on that form but he was laboured at Exeter last month and that took his hurdling record to 1-18.
runners.comment: In good form during the winter but he was laboured at Exeter latest; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Its A Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but he came from rear to finish second off a reduced mark over C&D (good to soft) last month; back up 2lb in the weights but he ran into an improver last time and he's still unexposed as a stayer; dangerous.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but was runner-up behind an improver over C&D last time; dangerous

runners.name: Walkinthepresent
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt over hurdles when when justifying favouritism at Taunton (3m, good) last month; up 7lb but he hit a clear personal best with that comfortable success and he's a major player again.
runners.comment: Hit a clear personal best when scoring at Taunton (3m, good) and he's a major player again

runners.name: Harry Junior
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he's 2-4 since switched to handicaps and that last success was after a break at Exeter (2m7f, good to soft) last month; up 5lb but he rallied well last time and is open to more progress at staying trips; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Record of 2-4 in handicaps and he rallied well at Exeter last time; respected up 5lb

runners.name: Stage Show
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 29th attempt (including Flat) when landing a handicap at Exeter (2m7f, good to firm) in October; failed to back that up at Taunton (2m3f, heavy) last month but that was under very different conditions and he may have needed the run; this looks more suitable back up in trip and he could go well with cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Won at Exeter on his penultimate run and he had an excuse on heavy latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Lydeard Girl
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on handicap debut at Hereford (2m6f, heavy) in February but she was beaten when she unseated there (2m3f, good) two weeks ago; bit to prove again after that and she needs improvement at this new trip.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden who unseated last time and she needs improvement at this new trip

runners.name: All Night Parking
runners.spotlight: Won two points in spring 2024 but he's failed to complete in last four runs including in hurdle handicaps (125-1) in his last two starts; still lightly raced in this sphere but he has a lot to prove back up in trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Has failed to complete in his last four runs including at 125-1 in two hurdle handicaps

races.name: Bridles Care Home In Wincanton Handicap Chase

runners.name: Rangatira Jack
runners.spotlight: Scored off 2lb higher at Exeter (2m3f, good) last April but he's been disappointing since and was a well-held sixth behind Kingcormac at Exeter last month; passed over.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb higher at Exeter last April but he's been disappointing since
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

runners.name: Hatos
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on heavy ground but he ran well on good to soft over C&D last month when runner-up behind Universal Secret; nudged up 2lb but he usually races prominently and should go well again.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner who was a creditable second over C&D last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Applicationofcash
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but he made it fourth time lucky over fences when scoring on his stable debut at Taunton (2m, good) last month; the leader made a bad mistake at the last but this 8yo wasn't far away at the time and he remains feasibly treated after a 2lb rise; respected back up in trip.
runners.comment: Looked fortunate at Taunton but he remains feasibly treated off 2lb higher; respected

runners.name: Kingcormac
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has won three of his last five starts and he hit a personal best when swooping late at Hereford (2m5f, good) two weeks ago; a 3lb rise for that success looks fair and he's strongly respected in his hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: 10yo who hit a personal best when completing a double at Hereford latest; key player

runners.name: Universal Secret
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in his first two starts this season but he returned after another wind operation with a win over C&D (good to soft; tongue-tie added; Hatos second) in February; up 6lb but he remains feasibly treated and has claims if he can back up that last run.
runners.comment: Returned after wind surgery with a C&D win (Hatos second) in February; respected up 6lb

runners.name: My Rockstar
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has won twice over fences but he faded into a well-held fourth behind Universal Secret over C&D in February; 3lb out of the weights in this rematch and he has work to do to turn the tables.
runners.comment: Well held behind Universal Secret over C&D latest and he's out of the weights here

runners.name: Move With The Beat
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-8 in points; made his breakthrough under rules when landing a handicap chase at Ludlow (2m4f, good to soft) in January but he was well held at Taunton the following month; has bit to prove again after that and he's now 1-7 over regulation fences; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Record of 5-8 in points but he's 1-11 under rules and was well held at Taunton last time

races.name: Bridles New Luxury Care Home Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Lake Tiberias
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo but he has form figures of 6666 over hurdles and was beaten 34l on handicap debut at Doncaster (2m3f, good to soft) in February; dropped 3lb but he needs a transformation after a short break.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 6666 over hurdles and he needs a transformation after a short break

runners.name: Walk In The West
runners.spotlight: Struggled in first three runs over hurdles but he kicked off his handicap career with a promising second behind Victory Shout at Taunton (2m, good) last week; was only beaten 2l in that race and he has a 7lb swing in the weights in this rematch; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Second behind Victory Shout on handicap debut at Taunton last week; respected in rematch

runners.name: Prince Rhinegold
runners.spotlight: Just one win from starts over hurdles but he returned after a short break with a close second here (1m7f, good to soft) last month; up 2lb but he seems effective at this trip and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Record of 1-18 over hurdles but he went close here last month; in the mix back up in trip
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

runners.name: Prairie Diamond
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper on good ground and a handicap hurdle (2m4f) on good to soft; ran right up to his best when third in a Taunton handicap in January but he was disappointing after wind surgery there last month; bit to prove again after that and he needs new headgear to work.
runners.comment: On workable mark but he flopped after wind surgery at Taunton last month; new headgear

runners.name: Mancero
runners.spotlight: Ex-French winning hurdler but he's not progressed for current yard and was tailed off in a Taunton handicap (3m, good) last month; still has low mileage but he needs a transformation back in trip.
runners.comment: Ex-French winning hurdler but he's struggled for current yard; needs a transformation

runners.name: Bread And Butter
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner (2m3f-2m5f) but his last success was 16 months ago and this veteran has been out of sorts in his last three runs; opposable.
runners.comment: His last win was 15 months ago and he's been out of sorts in last three runs; opposable

runners.name: Get In Order
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo who showed some promise in novice/maiden hurdles but he finished out the back on handicap debut at Warwick (2m, good to soft; 20-1) last month; half-brother to a 2m3f hurdle winner but he needs to kick on again at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo but he finished out the back on handicap debut at Warwick last month

runners.name: Sawpit Sienna
runners.spotlight: 11yo mare who has not won in over two years and was tailed off at Taunton (2m3f, good) last month; others are preferred.
runners.comment: 11yo who has not won in over two years and was tailed off at Taunton last month

runners.name: Eric Gorcombe
runners.spotlight: Poor in points and same story in first four runs over hurdles; much better effort when front-running fourth on handicap debut at Fontwell (2m5f, soft) last month but that was in a small field and this is a deeper race; needs to find more with cheekpeices added.
runners.comment: Better effort on handicap debut at Fontwell (2m5f) but he needs to build on that

runners.name: Athenas Dream
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in sole bumper but she's struggled at 22-1 or bigger in three hurdles runs; still early days but she needs improvement upped in trip on handicap debut; blinkers added.
runners.comment: 4yo filly who needs improvement upped in trip on handicap debut; blinkers added

runners.name: Forest Hills
runners.spotlight: 1m2f Flat winner who has been beaten 77l, 71l and 54l in three runs over hurdles this year; now goes handicapping off a lowly mark but he needs to show a lot more on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Makes handicap hurdle debut but he needs major improvement at this new trip

runners.name: Victory Shout
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful on the Flat at his best; didn't show much in first four hurdle runs but he really got his act together when landing a gamble in a Taunton handicap (2m, good) last Tuesday; has a penalty but that was a convincing win and he's respected on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Came good over hurdles when landing a gamble at Taunton last week; respected under penalty

races.name: Visit Bridles Care Home Wincanton Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Beau Speed
runners.spotlight: Â£110,000 3yo and half-brother to five winners including Authorised Speed (useful bumper and 2m/2m3f hurdle); big market drifter out to 11-2 at Fakenham (2m, soft) in February but he ran well to finish a close second behind an odds-on rival; sets a fair standard on that debut form and he should improve for the experience.
runners.comment: Big market drifter but he was a promising second on Fakenham debut; key player

runners.name: Crocks Bottom
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam useful 2m4f-3m3f hurdle/chase winner, sister to useful bumper/2m-3m hurdle winner Cresswell Legend, out of 2m6f-3m chase winner; yard 10% in bumpers this season and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Out of a useful 2m4f-3m3f jumps winner; market should reveal expectations on debut

runners.name: Lord Churchill
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to three winners including Sonnyboyliston (1m-1m6f Flat including Ebor/Irish St Leger, RPR 121; 2m hurdle) and Lot Of Joy (1m4f-2m Flat, 101; useful 2m-2m2f hurdle); dam 1m7f-2m Flat (78) and 2m3f/2m4f hurdle winner; plenty to like on paper but yard has a modest strike-rate in bumpers (2-75) in recent years.
runners.comment: Plenty to like on paper but yard has a modest strike-rate in bumpers in recent years

runners.name: Posh Chick
runners.spotlight: Out of a prolific point and 2m6f/3m chase winner; won on point debut (3m, good to soft) in February 2025 but was pulled up on her comeback in November; has something to prove now switched to rules after another break and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won her first point but was pulled in the second one; bit to prove after another break

runners.name: Beau Merle
runners.spotlight: 24,000euros 3yo; first foal; dam French 2m1f hurdle winner, half-sister to French 2m1f chase winner Bertie, out of French 2m2f/2m4f hurdle winner; yard has had a debut bumper winner this season and interesting to see how he figures in market.
runners.comment: 24,000euros yearling; out of French hurdle winner and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Blackmore Jack
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, sister to bumper/2m1f hurdle winner Bobs Lord Tara and 3m hurdle winner Skiddaw Tara, closely related to 2m1f hurdle winner Lady Samback; has fair target to aim at on debut and yard is not known for bumper winners.
runners.comment: Has fair target to aim at on debut and yard is not know for bumper winners
runners.owner: The Cant Take It With You Syndicate

runners.name: Martial Law
runners.spotlight: 6,000euros foal; second foal; dam point/3m hurdle winner, out of unraced half-sister to 2m2f hurdle winner Long Walk Home; has decent standard to aim at on debut and he could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Has decent standard to aim at on debut and he could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Lissahane Lass
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a bumper winner but was 80-1 on Huntingdon debut (2m, good) last month and she finished a well-held seventh of 14; opposable.
runners.comment: 80-1 on Huntingdon debut last month and she finished a well-held seventh of 14

races.name: Book The Bistro At Southwell Racecourse Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Always Busy
runners.spotlight: Hasn't obviously been shaping as if likely to strike again (pulled up in Chepstow race Fern Hill was second in last time) but the fact he won over C&D on good off an identical mark last spring makes him a dangerous one to dismiss.
runners.comment: Recent efforts not great but he did win over C&D on good off this mark last spring

runners.name: Queens Wish
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D novice handicap (good to soft) in November on her first chase run for present connections but nowhere near as good as her two thirds since might imply, dropping out tamely to be last of the finishers at Market Rasen just 17 days ago; a return to Southwell needs to spark a quick revival.
runners.comment: C&D win in November but well below that level when twice third since

runners.name: Fern Hill
runners.spotlight: Two small-field wins around 3m on good in the autumn; went off the boil subsequently but very much back on his game when second of 11 at Chepstow 17 days ago; raised 5lb for pulling clear of the rest but it looks justified given the third was successful back at Chepstow last week.
runners.comment: Two 3m wins on good in autumn; back on form when second latest; respected despite 5lb rise
runners.owner: J E Harrison & Mrs S Case

runners.name: Hell Hound
runners.spotlight: Successful in a bumper (good) and course maiden hurdle (good to soft) in the first half of 2024; bettered that winning course form when twice reaching the frame over hurdles much later that year; pulled up when switched to fences for his comeback run at Taunton seven weeks ago but it's possible the heavy ground was against him given his form has come under less testing conditions.
runners.comment: Course hurdle winner; heavy ground possibly didn't suit when pulled up on chase debut
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate- Hell Hound

runners.name: Noche Negra
runners.spotlight: Jumped well when scoring on his stable debut at Warwick (2m4f, soft) in late 2024 but he hasn't recaptured that form in three runs following a year's absence, pulling up on his last two starts.
runners.comment: Won on yard debut in late 2024 but hasn't shown much in just three runs since

races.name: Easter Family Fun Day 5th April Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Big Boy Barney
runners.spotlight: This Irish point winner was having only his second chase start when landing a cosy success in a small-field Huntingdon handicap (2m4f, good) three weeks ago; seems to have odd quirk (hung left on the run-in at Huntingdon) but has the potential for better again over fences.
runners.comment: Point winner who made it second-time lucky over fences at Huntingdon latest; may do better

runners.name: Ukantango
runners.spotlight: Back from a break to register a third win over hurdles at Hereford (2m, soft) in January; not in the same form in a bigger field at Warwick since but he could be well treated back chasing, having twice finished runner-up off marks in the low 120s in a five-race spell over fences between November 2023 and May 2024.
runners.comment: Hurdle win this winter and placed off much higher marks in his previous spell chasing

runners.name: Gilbertina
runners.spotlight: Yet to win over fences but has performed with credit when reaching the frame on her last two starts, latterly third of eight over 2m4f at Newcastle (good to soft) four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Yet to win over fences but has recorded creditable in-frame efforts in recent months

runners.name: Kayce Dutton
runners.spotlight: Two Market Rasen chase wins since last May but pulled up there in February and well-held fifth of six over 3m here since, taking his Southwell record to 0-7.
runners.comment: Two Market Rasen chase wins but pulled up there in February and remote fifth here since

runners.name: Intenzo
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who made the frame in three maiden hurdles for his new stable in January/February and finished second when switched to fences for his handicap debut at Sedgefield (2m, good) three weeks ago; open to progress now stepping back up in trip.
runners.comment: Second on 2m chase/handicap debut last month and open to progress now back up in trip

runners.name: Boston Boy
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up and maiden hurdle winner but has disappointed in three chase starts this winter, pulling up at Sedgefield with cheekpieces added eight weeks ago; blinkers are tried now.
runners.comment: No show in three chases this winter, pulling up in cheekpieces latest; blinkers on now

races.name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Mares' Standard NH Flat Race (Cond'/Am') (Cat 1) (GBB)

runners.name: Queens Charm
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£60,000 after winning a 3m Irish point on soft in November; her new stable does well in bumpers, including a win in this race last year; obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Point winner who makes obvious appeal for last year's winning yard

runners.name: Frost Moon
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of Irish point last spring; 13-2 and hooded, never a threat from the rear when eighth of 14 in Ludlow bumper in December (2m, good to soft) but more than hinted at ability and could improve with that behind her.
runners.comment: Point winner; never dangerous on bumper debut but in top hands so may do better

runners.name: Rose Of Knocknamoe
runners.spotlight: 13l second of three finishers in a 3m Irish point (soft) in December; good new stable has a healthy 10-58 bumper record this season; it'll be interesting to see how strong the market vibes are.
runners.comment: Second in 3m Irish point in December; new stable does well in bumpers

runners.name: Aunt Gallie
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Ragmans Corner (1m6f Flat, RPR 86; 2m hurdle) and Longview Lady (bumper); the stable is 0-14 in bumpers in the current campaign but had a smart performer in this sphere last season.
runners.comment: Stable 0-14 in bumpers this season but had a smart performer in this sphere last term

runners.name: Rosies Honour
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Charterhouse, a 1m Listed winner on the Flat in Ireland; dam a useful winner around 1m6f on the Flat; the betting should help guide to expectations for a stable no stranger to bumper success.
runners.comment: Betting should help guide to expectations for a stable no stranger to bumper success

runners.name: Frandefo
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to French 2m1f-2m3f hurdle winner Grandbat; yard 1-12 in bumpers in the last five seasons; a watching brief is the percentage call.
runners.comment: Probably best watched for yard which is 1-12 in bumpers in last five seasons

runners.name: Just Rizzo
runners.spotlight: Closely related/half-sister to a few winners (including points), notably useful 2m3f-2m5f hurdle winner The White Mouse; dam also a point winner; bred to have a future but it's a pedigree which suggests she may require a stiffer stamina test than these races tend to provide.
runners.comment: Bred to have a future but might be more one for longer trips down the line

runners.name: No More Teardrops
runners.spotlight: A very likely type on paper, being the first foal of 2021 Cheltenham Mares' Hurdle winner Black Tears (also a smart bumper performer).
runners.comment: A very likely type on paper, being first foal of 2021 Mares' Hurdle winner Black Tears

runners.name: Greyse
runners.spotlight: 250-1 when tailed off on Huntingdon debut just three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 250-1 when tailed off on Huntingdon debut just three weeks ago
runners.owner: D S Frankland & D J Trott

runners.name: Briery Bluebelle
runners.spotlight: 11-1, pulled up on Huntingdon debut (soft) ten weeks ago; a subsequent breathing operation needs to have made a big difference.
runners.comment: Had a breathing operation since pulling up on debut in January
runners.owner: Mrs Helen Plumbly & Mrs Amanda Mantell

races.name: Country And Western Night 25th April Maiden Hurdle (Novices' Championship Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Road To Wembley
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful four-time AW Flat winner in these silks for Richard Hughes and promising second of ten on his Warwick hurdle debut (2m, good to soft) for Olly Murphy 24 days ago; entitled to improve and the one to beat.
runners.comment: Four Flat wins and promising second on recent hurdle debut for new yard; the one to beat

runners.name: Top Coeur
runners.spotlight: Fair form when reaching the frame in a bumper and two maiden hurdles (all 2m) in Ireland for Gavin Cromwell last year but weakened quickly from two out when a remote last of six in 2m4f Hereford handicap for new yard a fortnight ago; quickly drops back in trip.
runners.comment: Fair form in bumper/hurdles over 2m in Ireland but tailed off over 2m4f on yard debut

runners.name: Corsican Caper
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m Flat winner; tailed off on his first attempt over hurdles in January but showed an aptitude for it when third of four at Doncaster (2m, good to soft) 19 days ago; he'll likely require another step forward to take this, though.
runners.comment: Fair on Flat; translated that ability to hurdles when third latest; more needed again

runners.name: Papillondelumiere
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden in the pointing field and not seen since finishing third over 2m4f in May 2024; this 8yo would be a surprise winner on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden in points and off for 676 days ahead of belated hurdle debut

runners.name: Lou Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Well held in two bumpers ahead of this switch to hurdles; outsider.
runners.comment: Well held in two bumpers ahead of this switch to hurdles; outsider
runners.owner: Prof Alyn Morice & Miss Caroline Nowell

runners.name: Paragon
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden on Flat; reached the frame on the first of two hurdle outings at Huntingdon in recent months but the form is barely modest; more needed.
runners.comment: Modest maiden on Flat and similar form in two hurdles at Huntingdon in recent months

runners.name: Promethean
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f Flat scorer for David Menuisier but yet to fire for his current yard, including in two hurdles; can't make a case for.
runners.comment: Fair Flat winner for David Menuisier but yet to fire for this yard, including two hurdles

runners.name: Novelista
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat for Charlie Johnston, proving reliable and winning 2m handicap at Goodwood (heavy) last autumn (sold for 165,000gns soon after); 9-2, weakened to finish a well-held sixth of ten in Ascot juvenile (2m, soft) on hurdle debut in January; should be capable of a lot better in this sphere.
runners.comment: Useful on Flat; well-held sixth on hurdle debut but should be capable of better

races.name: Southwell Racecourse Conference And Events Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ocean Conquest
runners.spotlight: Completed a good-ground double at Ludlow (2m novice/2m5f handicap) in the autumn; disappointed on next two starts but took a step back in the right direction when fifth of nine at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft) 24 days ago; below par on his previous attempt at 3m but he's back to only 1lb above his winning mark and today's ground conditions should suit so there are reasons to be hopeful.
runners.comment: Two good-ground wins in autumn; fair fifth latest; claims if stamina holds back up in trip

runners.name: Diamond Geezer
runners.spotlight: Struck in first-time cheekpieces at Perth (2m4f, soft) in October and returned to winning ways at Ayr (extended 2m4f, soft) in January; only seventh back there since and now swaps the cheekpieces for first-time blinkers; below par on his previous outing around 3m but wasn't obviously beaten for stamina.
runners.comment: Two soft-ground wins in cheekpieces; below par latest and blinkers are tried now

runners.name: Campaign Trail
runners.spotlight: Made a sound return to hurdles after four months off when fourth of nine over 2m7f at Market Rasen six weeks ago, fading only from the last under a positive ride; remains unexposed as a stayer in this sphere (quite useful over 2m on the Flat); considered.
runners.comment: Faded only late back from a break latest; remains unexposed as a stayer over hurdles

runners.name: El Galactico
runners.spotlight: Won two four-runner novice hurdles on good ground last year, the first over just short of 3m; fair fifth on 2m5f Ascot handicap debut in November but weakened quickly when pulled up over 3m at Doncaster back from a break 19 days ago; first-time cheekpieces are reached for on the back of that.
runners.comment: Lightly raced dual good-ground winner but ran poorly latest; cheekpieces are added now
runners.owner: Feilim O Muiri And Brendan Barrett

runners.name: Passing Pleasure
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Musselburgh (hurdle; 2m4f, soft) and Catterick (chase; 2m3f, good to soft) on first two outings for this yard; beaten at short odds but advanced his RPR again when stepped up to around 3m under rules for the first time in a Newcastle chase four weeks ago, going down by a neck to a next-time-out winner with the pair 16l clear; another 6lb higher back hurdling but it may not stop him, particularly if reacting well to the new cheekpieces (added to the tongue-tie he's worn since joining this stable).
runners.comment: Progressive for this yard, winning twice and second; cheekpieces added; bold show likely

runners.name: Jubilant
runners.spotlight: Third chase success when scoring by 5l at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft) in December; stamina seemingly stretched by 3m5f back from a short break at Warwick 24 days ago; slightly lower mark back hurdling.
runners.comment: Third chase win in December; 3m5f seemingly stretched him since; lower mark back hurdling

runners.name: Moon Hunter
runners.spotlight: Useful in his prime for Henry Daly but left that yard on a low note; soundly beaten in chases on his first two starts for this stable but took a step back in the right direction returned to hurdles in first-time blinkers when rallying third of eight over the extended 2m4f at Plumpton (good to soft) 23 days ago; needs to prove he can build on that with the blinkers left off in favour of refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction back hurdling latest; needs to show he can build on that now

runners.name: Immortal Fame
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner who has raced exclusively over fences since joining Deborah Cole in the middle of 2024, scoring a further four times, including over 2m4f here last season; off the mark for the current campaign when shading a close finish at Market Rasen (3m, soft) in February but pulled up when favourite at Hereford since and now back over timber.
runners.comment: Pulled up latest and now returns to hurdles for first time since joining this yard in 2024

races.name: Join Southwell Golf Club Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Princesse Milania
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for this trainer at Wetherby (2m5f, good) in October; hasn't really built on that since (beaten 17l when second over just short of 3m at Newcastle latest) but this is a weak race and being dropped another 2lb can only help.
runners.comment: Hasn't really built on yard debut win in October, beaten 17l when second latest

runners.name: Shorten Sassy
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who isn't obviously getting closer to a first handicap success despite falling in the weights, beaten 34l into fifth over 3m1f at Huntingdon last time.
runners.comment: Isn't obviously getting closer to a first success despite falling in the weights

runners.name: Briery Butterfly
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but has finished second or third in handicaps on all four starts this season; this trip/ground fine and she's likely to go well again.
runners.comment: Consistent in defeat in handicaps this season and likely to be on the premises again
runners.owner: Mrs H Plumbly J Trafford K Deane S Holme

runners.name: Belladinotte
runners.spotlight: Second over C&D and went one better at Hexham (both good) last June on her first two starts for Sara Ender; back on form when runner-up twice at Wetherby (3m/2m5f, good) in the autumn and given a break since finishing fourth on softish ground at Market Rasen in November; should have a big say if ready to roll after 139 days off.
runners.comment: Has a much more solid record than most of these and big player if primed after break

runners.name: Rosa Diaz
runners.spotlight: Won her only Irish point; fifth in 2m5f Ludlow novice hurdle on debut under rules but she's yet to reproduce that, including seventh of eight on handicap hurdle debut at Huntingdon (2m4f, good; 9-1) last time when the cheekpieces joined her usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Yet to build on hurdle debut, including in a handicap

runners.name: Mya Du Clos
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in her three qualifying runs around 2m (good to soft/heavy) this winter but her yard has been among the winners in recent days and she's one to monitor closely in the betting now handicapping over a significantly longer trip.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three hurdles around 2m but more chance now handicapping up in trip

runners.name: Venturevango
runners.spotlight: Has achieved little across her six starts (first five in Ireland), including pulling up when 28-1 for her recent Sedgefield stable debut over 2m4f; now steps up in trip with the cheekpieces she wore on her final start in Ireland back in place; could only consider if backed.
runners.comment: No form of worth, including pulling up on recent yard debut; could only consider if backed

races.name: Sedgefield Racecourse Supports Autism In Racing Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Captain Cool
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over C&D in October and advanced his form (refitted with cheekpieces) when a clear second of nine back over C&D 22 days ago; up 5lb but the winner has gone in again so he's a player for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Advanced his form when clear second over C&D 22 days ago; a player for his in-form yard

runners.name: Belaya River
runners.spotlight: A fair staying maiden on the Flat for Charlie Fellowes; has made a positive start in this sphere, scoring at Catterick (2m) in January and backing it up with a good second of seven in 2m Kelso juvenile 32 days ago; not out of things now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Good second in 2m Kelso juvenile 32 days ago; not out of things now going into handicaps

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: Opened her account at Bangor (2m) last May but below par since (mainly on the Flat); well-held fourth over C&D on latest hurdling start last month; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Opened her account at Bangor last May but below par since (mainly on Flat); more is needed

runners.name: Ambassador
runners.spotlight: Scored at Cartmel (2m1f) in August but largely out of sorts subsequently, pulled up in 2m3f Carlisle handicap in November; lots more is needed after a layoff.
runners.comment: Scored at Cartmel in August but pulled up at Carlisle in November; lots more required

runners.name: Diamond Jim
runners.spotlight: Emphatic winner at Haydock (2m) in December and not discredited when fourth of seven in 2m Warwick juvenile the following month; cheekpieces go for his handicap debut after a break and he needs considering.
runners.comment: Solid fourth at Warwick latest; cheekpieces on for h'cap debut and needs considering

runners.name: Maple
runners.spotlight: Failed to kick on over hurdles after scoring at Uttoxeter (2m) last May but he comes here on the back of a good second on the Flat at Newcastle three weeks ago; must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Failed to kick on over hurdles but good second on Flat recently; enters calculations
runners.owner: R Whitelock L Clemitson & R Jefferson

runners.name: Fine Point
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough victory at Kempton (2m) last April but he was largely below par after for Nigel Hawke, only tenth in 2m Uttoxeter handicap hurdle last July; returns after eight months off for a yard well among the winners, though, so no surprise to see him play a more prominent role.
runners.comment: Ended time for Nigel Hawke below par; could bounce back for in-form new stable after break

races.name: Sign Lead Your Horse To Water Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mountain Mike
runners.spotlight: Quick to show fairly useful (winning) form over hurdles and he's made a promising start in this sphere after 13 months off, runner-up in 3m1f handicap at Hereford 25 days ago; this lightly raced son of Mount Nelson looks to have more to offer; merits serious consideration eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Good runner-up at Hereford last month; more to offer so merits serious consideration

runners.name: Realisation
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort who capitalised on a slipping mark when getting off the mark over C&D 22 days ago; back up 6lb but she can go well again.
runners.comment: Reliable sort who got off the mark over C&D 22 days ago; up 6lb but she can go well again

runners.name: Ailt An Chorrain
runners.spotlight: Yet to score in this sphere but has made the frame on both starts since fitted with blinkers, albeit remote when third of six in 3m1f Catterick handicap chase 36 days ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: In the frame on both starts since fitted with blinkers; possibilities

runners.name: Stratagem
runners.spotlight: A very useful 3m chase winner at his best for Paul Nicholls; not so good nowadays but this veteran wasn't disgraced on the back of breathing surgery when third of nine in 3m Doncaster handicap chase 33 days ago; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when third in Doncaster handicap chase 33 days ago; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Mary Stanford
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when a debut hurdling second at Hexham in October 2024 but very lightly raced since and has failed to build on it, beating only one on his handicap debut at Newcastle (2m4f) in January; this winning pointer could still do better now going chasing though.
runners.comment: Failed to go on over hurdles; this winning pointer could still do better now going chasing

races.name: Jayne And Greg Complete All The Racecourses Handicap Chase

runners.name: Dixie Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in this sphere over C&D in November and three times a runner-up since, latest (cheekpieces on) in 2m1f handicap at Market Rasen 17 days ago; big shout off an unchanged mark with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Good second at Market Rasen latest; big shout off an unchanged mark with headgear retained

runners.name: Clean Getaway
runners.spotlight: A two-time C&D scorer in 2024 but his more recent form is far less encouraging, last of five back over C&D 22 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: A two-time C&D scorer in 2024 but his more recent form is far less encouraging

runners.name: Natural Ability
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in November 2024 but there's not been much to enthuse over since, cheekpieces fitted when a well-held third of five in 2m Catterick handicap chase four weeks ago; he needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November 2024 but there's not been much to enthuse over since

runners.name: Restandbethankful
runners.spotlight: Course winner but it's now 16 starts since his last victory and he came in a remote fifth of seven over C&D on Boxing Day; he's not one to rely on.
runners.comment: Course winner but a remote fifth over C&D on Boxing Day; he's not one to rely on

runners.name: Elusive Warrior
runners.spotlight: Only poor form shown in his five runs over hurdles, tried in a hood when pulled up at Newcastle (2m4f) 18 days ago; goes chasing with tongue tie added and lots to prove.
runners.comment: Only poor form shown in his five runs over hurdles; goes chasing with lots to prove
runners.owner: S McMillan M Gabellone P Thompson

runners.name: Myfavouritesister
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden hurdler but she's been off the track since posting a solid third in 2m4f Perth handicap in September; yard is going well so she's no forlorn hope starting out in this sphere.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden hurdler; off since September but no forlorn hope now going chasing

runners.name: Beep Beep Becky
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish point and she hinted at promise in a light campaign over hurdles before beating just one home on her handicap debut at Catterick (3m1f) four weeks ago; in good hands and could take a step forward now going chasing with a tongue tie added.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise over hurdles; could take step forward now chasing with tongue tie on

runners.name: Nonbinding
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 2m-winning hurdler for Gordon Elliott in 2022 but very lightly raced subsequently and has offered little for his current yard, pulled up over C&D 11 months ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Has offered little for current yard, pulled up over C&D 11 months ago; hard to warm to

races.name: Fairlight Studios Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bright Legend
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful maiden hurdler/chaser for Denis Hogan; went too keenly on his first three outings for his current yard but bounced back to form when (refitted with tongue strap) third of 15 in 2m4f Wincanton handicap 35 days ago; the clear form pick back in maiden company; headgear retained.
runners.comment: Back to form with Wincanton third latest; the clear form pick back in maiden company

runners.name: Whos Glen
runners.spotlight: A useful winning stayer on the Flat for Andrew Balding; yet to match that form in this sphere but he posted his best effort when third of ten in 2m4f handicap at Musselburgh 12 days ago; not out of things on this stiffer track.
runners.comment: Best effort hurdling when third at Musselburgh 12 days ago; not out of things

runners.name: Hay Max
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when a debut second in Bangor bumper last April but has twice failed to build on it in similar company, off five months before fifth at Huntingdon last month; more is required now going hurdling.
runners.comment: Debut second in Bangor bumper but twice failed to build on it; more needed now hurdling

runners.name: Phantom Gold
runners.spotlight: Point bumper winner; hooded when easily landing bumper at Newton Abbot in July 2023 for Ben Brookhouse; absent since but still needs considering on his yard/hurdling debut, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Easily landed Newton Abbot bumper in July 2023; needs considering on yard/hurdling debut

runners.name: Monark Wood
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied when runner-up in a 2m1f claiming hurdle in France for David Cottin in November but he beat only one home on his yard debut in 2m Huntingdon juvenile last month; hooded (tongue tie also refitted) here so could take a step forward.
runners.comment: French recruit but beat only one on yard at Huntingdon last month; hooded/tongue tied here

runners.name: Fearless Dragon
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced maiden who came in a remote third over C&D in February; cheekpieces on when unseated rider at the third in a 2m novice here 22 days ago and he needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Remote C&D third & unseated rider at the 3rd here since; needs to take a big step forward
runners.owner: N Jolley J Barnard & C Kennedy

races.name: MW Civils & Groundworks Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Cosmic Soul
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with a Flat success at Southwell and he's bagged back-to-back handicaps here since, readily landing the odds over C&D 13 days ago; hiked up 10lb now but thriving and still the one to beat.
runners.comment: Readily landed the odds over C&D 13 days ago; hiked up 10lb now but still the one to beat
runners.owner: M Lee D Lee C Jolley & Neville

runners.name: Laddie Cooper Mia
runners.spotlight: Still to get her head in front but she's made a positive start for Adam Nicol this term, runner-up to Cosmic Soul over C&D 13 days ago; she enjoys a 9lb pull in the weights now so this keen-going sort is a player.
runners.comment: Good second to Cosmic Soul over C&D; has a 9lb pull so this keen-going sort is a player

runners.name: Top Flight Century
runners.spotlight: Made a winning yard debut over C&D in December but he was pulled up in 2m4f handicap at Newcastle only run since; he has a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Made a winning yard debut over C&D but pulled up at Newcastle since; has it to prove now

runners.name: Byron Hill
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and he was tried in a change of headgear when a below-form fourth of eight in 2m1f handicap here 13 days ago; this return to a longer trip should suit though so he's no forlorn hope from a slipping mark.
runners.comment: On long losing sequence; this return to a longer trip should suit so he's no forlorn hope

runners.name: Swingforthefence
runners.spotlight: Son of Soldier Of Fortune who was fitted with cheekpieces but failed to build on earlier promise when sixth of seven on handicap debut over C&D 22 days ago; he's in good hands though and might be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Failed to build on earlier promise when 6th over C&D latest; might be worth another chance

runners.name: Knacker Trapper
runners.spotlight: It's now 11 outings since his last success but he's been lightly raced this term and comes here on the back of a good third of 15 in 2m4f Newcastle handicap hurdle 18 days ago; can make his presence felt off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good third at Newcastle 18 days ago; can make his presence felt off the same mark

runners.name: Kopa Kilana
runners.spotlight: Is 0-29 over hurdles and he ran poorly in a refitted tongue tie when ninth of 11 in 2m Wetherby handicap hurdle 15 days ago; it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Is 0-29 over hurdles and only ninth at Wetherby 15 days ago; it's easy to look elsewhere

runners.name: Mr Globalist
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but he arrives in decent nick, fifth of 11 over C&D 13 days ago; he's 5lb out of the handicap here though.
runners.comment: Remains winless but he arrives in decent nick; 5lb out of the handicap here though

runners.name: Achnamara
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who has yet to hit top form this season, a below-par fifth on the Flat at Newcastle three weeks ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: C&D scorer who has yet to hit top form this season; others are preferred

runners.name: Deauville Lady
runners.spotlight: Has cut little ice in a trio of runs over hurdles, coming in a well-held sixth of nine in 2m Taunton novice 48 days ago; she needs this switch to handicaps to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Has cut little ice in three hurdles runs; needs switch to h'caps to spark major progress

runners.name: Seeyainthesuninn
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler; has offered little in his two runs this term, only eighth of ten over C&D in November; cheekpieces are reached for after a layoff.
runners.comment: Has offered little in two runs this term, eighth over C&D in November; cheekpieces go on

races.name: Paul Richardson Visiting Sedgefield Today Open National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 1 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Connells Cross
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Fascinating Rock who built on a promising debut second for Fergal O'Brien last May when cosily landing four-runner bumper over C&D 22 days ago; she looks to have more to offer and holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Cosily landed bumper over C&D 22 days ago; looks to have more to offer; leading claims

runners.name: The Gem State
runners.spotlight: Purchased for Â£75,000 after finishing runner-up in a point in November; this son of Idaho is in very good hands and rates an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Purchased for Â£75,000 after finishing second in a point in November; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Datstheholyallofit
runners.spotlight: 10-1, well-held last of four to Connells Cross in C&D bumper 22 days ago; this Snow Sky mare needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Last of four in C&D bumper 22 days ago; this Snow Sky mare needs to take big step forward

runners.name: Dolly Dior
runners.spotlight: Â£1,000 4yo; Sir Percy mare; half-sister to Flat winners Vondelpark (1m; RPR 73) and Lethal Touch (9.4f/1m2f; RPR 71); dam, unraced half-sister to useful 7f/1m2f 2yo winner Al Ghazal, out of sister to top miler Cape Cross; the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Sir Percy mare; the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this newcomer

runners.name: Mords Lavie Spigao
runners.spotlight: Tunis gelding; 32,000euros 3yo; dam, French 1m4f AQPS Flat winner, is a half-sister to 1m4f AQPS Flat/2m hurdle winner Khrisma; possibilities on his debut.
runners.comment: Tunis gelding; possibilities on his debut

runners.name: Smart Order
runners.spotlight: Dam is an unraced half-sister to smart/prolific bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Lady Buttons, out of unraced close relative of useful bumper and 2m hurdle/chase winner Royal Alphabet; this Order Of St George gelding can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: This Order Of St George gelding can't be ruled out

runners.name: Hillsteels Blue
runners.spotlight: Califet gelding; dam, French 2m1f hurdle winner, is out of a French 2m2f Grade 3 hurdle winner; the market can guide on his debut.
runners.comment: Califet gelding; the market can guide on his debut

runners.name: Scopic
runners.spotlight: Dam is an unraced sister to point/2m hurdle winner Leeswood Lily; this Telescope gelding needs considering for his in-form stable.
runners.comment: This Telescope gelding needs considering for his in-form stable.

races.name: Virgin Bet Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Nanoscience
runners.spotlight: Progressed with each start as a juvenile, finishing third over 7f at Newcastle (AW debut) when last seen in October; in top hands and likely has more to offer this year.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive last year and likely more to come for top yard in 2026

runners.name: Light Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Cost 90,000euros as a yearling and is a half-sister to five winners, notably Bolthole (1m-1m3f including 2yo/Qatar/French Listed; RPR 116); one to note in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a French Listed winner and needs monitoring in the betting on debut

runners.name: Divine Wish
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on her debut when 3.5l fourth of 12 over C&D in January; may do better again but it's likely she'll need to.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut when fair fourth over C&D in January; may do better again

runners.name: Lightkiss
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns half-sister to 7f/1m winner Many Tears (including AW Listed; RPR 102) and three Italian winners; from a good stable and would enter calculations if the betting speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: From a good stable and would enter calculations if the betting speaks in her favour.

runners.name: Rajwaah
runners.spotlight: By Pinatubo and the third foal of an unraced sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Nezwaah; doesn't have too high a standard to aim at on debut for a yard no stranger to first-time-out success.
runners.comment: Interesting newcomer for a yard no stranger to first-time-out success

runners.name: Blue Birdie
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Flying Fairies (7f-11.6f including Listed; RPR 102) and Tirso De Molina (French/Spanish 1m3f/11.5f); dam unraced sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Meathlusing; bred to have a future but probably best watched this time.
runners.comment: Bred to have a future but probably best watched this time

runners.name: Society Charm
runners.spotlight: 33-1, slowly away and beaten 12l when sixth of ten on 7f Chelmsford debut 20 days ago; needs to have come on a lot.
runners.comment: 33-1 when well-held sixth on last month's Chelmsford debut

runners.name: Music Academy
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and no obvious promise on 1m Yarmouth debut (good to soft) last autumn but this daughter of Kingman is from a good family and cost 600,000gns as a yearling; no surprise were she to prove a totally different proposition now.
runners.comment: Well held on 1m turf debut last autumn but in top hands and could leave that behind now

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap

runners.name: Billy Mill
runners.spotlight: Returned from a break to win a 7f handicap here, his third course success, in January; not in the same form since but he is now back to his last winning mark.
runners.comment: 7f win here off this mark in January; hasn't matched it in four runs since

runners.name: Havana Touch
runners.spotlight: Back to form when second of 11 at Wolverhampton (8.6f) a fortnight ago; 2lb higher today but Rossa Ryan booked and this course winner should have a big run in him.
runners.comment: Back to form when 2nd at Wolverhampton last month; 2lb rise to deal with; still respected

runners.name: Penfolds Grange
runners.spotlight: Two good runs have followed a wind operation in December, notably when headed in the final stride over C&D five weeks ago; 2lb higher today but still feasibly weighted.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts following a wind op in December, beaten a nose over C&D latest; chance

runners.name: Beta Reader
runners.spotlight: Began 2026 with two good placed efforts over 1m2f at Lingfield; easy enough to strike a line through latest effort as he ended up poorly positioned in a race run at a stop-start tempo; drops in trip and steps up in class today; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Two good runs (1m2f) to kick off the year; had excuses last time; up in class/down in trip

runners.name: Nordic Norm
runners.spotlight: Still to add to his debut success (for Karl Burke) but he went desperately close at Chelmsford last month, running on well at the finish; handicap mark left unchanged and he needs serious consideration.
runners.comment: Just missed out at Chelmsford last month; solid contender off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Chico Dulce
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who won over this trip at Lingfield in November; not so good off this mark when last seen in December; up in class today.
runners.comment: 1m win in 0-55 company in November; not so good one month later; up in class today

runners.name: Caph Star
runners.spotlight: Useful form, including a C&D novice win, back in 2022; absent for 1,218 days prior to December's reappearance (for Gordon Treacy); did enough to think she retained ability when eighth of 13 over C&D two weeks ago (stable debut); eased in grade and could yet come good again.
runners.comment: C&D win in 2022; some promise on stable debut two weeks ago; eased in grade today

runners.name: King Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on his 3yo form but 2025 was disappointing and he finished tailed off at Lingfield (1m2f) in January; tongue-tie absent today.
runners.comment: Thrown in on 3yo form but struggled last year and he was tailed off in January

runners.name: Magical Merlin
runners.spotlight: He finished second in three of his four starts in 2025, including on his seasonal return, but was still operating at a level below his old form; drops into 0-70 for the first time; not discounted.
runners.comment: On a losing run but ran some good races in defeat last year; drops into first 0-70 today

races.name: Virgin Bet Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Magic Trip
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Bath (1m, good to soft) last September before 8l last of eight in Italian Group 3 (7.5f, good) in October; gelded since and returns with yard in good form; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won on debut at Bath last September and tough task next time; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Balzac
runners.spotlight: Backed at big odds when third of seven at 22-1 on debut in quality maiden at Newmarket (1m, good) last September; this half-brother to the stable's 1m2f Group 3 winner Claymore is entered in the Derby and could have plenty more to offer; strong contender.
runners.comment: Third on debut in quality maiden at Newmarket last September and he's a strong contender

runners.name: Madame Passant
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros foal; fifth foal; Muhaarar half-sister to winners Oneonechop (French 6f-1m) and Al Et Chop (French 7f); dam unraced half-sister to Listed-placed 5f-1m2f winner; makes debut in a hood; watching brief advised.
runners.comment: 8,000euros foal; half-sister to two French winners; watching brief advised on debut

runners.name: Winding Stream
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 6-5 favourite when winning in good style on debut at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last October; entered in the Derby and this likeable prospect could play a leading role under a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism in good style on debut at Southwell last October; likeable prospect

runners.name: Fizzy Bear
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns foal, 30,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; fourth foal; Kodiac brother to US/Canadian Graded stakes-placed 5.5f 2yo/7f turf winner Appraise (RPR 108); dam 6.5f-1m1f winner (including Listed); he might be one for further down the line although a market check is advised on debut.
runners.comment: Potential in pedigree; might be one for further down the line but check betting on debut

runners.name: Claytons Kolatonic
runners.spotlight: He's been an outsider on both starts and has shown signs of inexperience when well beaten each time; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: An outsider on both starts and has shown signs of inexperience when well beaten each time

runners.name: West Hill Rosie
runners.spotlight: Failed to settle and tailed off at 25-1 on last month's debut in a bumper at Wincanton; probably best watched now switched to the Flat.
runners.comment: Failed to settle and tailed off at 25-1 on last month's debut in a bumper at Wincanton

runners.name: Bu Hail
runners.spotlight: His dam was a useful AW performer and his best days should lie ahead of him; however, if he's to threaten today he has to produce considerably more than he's managed on his first two starts (7f, good/AW).
runners.comment: His dam was a useful AW performer but improvement is needed on his third start

runners.name: Tiny Tiger
runners.spotlight: Connections have enjoyed success with her family but she showed no immediate promise when well beaten at 33-1 on recent debut at Lingfield.
runners.comment: She showed no immediate promise when well beaten at 33-1 on recent debut at Lingfield

runners.name: Alembic
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always in rear when 9-2 for recent debut at Southwell (1m, Tapeta); however, he has an excellent pedigree and it will be interesting to see if he's prominent in the betting once more.
runners.comment: Always in rear on recent debut at Southwell but has an excellent pedigree; watch betting

runners.name: Cribbins
runners.spotlight: Down the field in both bumpers and same story when 150-1 for his two Flat starts (1m4f/9.4f, AW).
runners.comment: Down the field in both bumpers; same story when 150-1 for his two Flat starts (1m4f/9.4f)

runners.name: Dijon Gone
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when 11l sixth of seven on debut at Bath (1m, soft) last October; however, that performance wasn't devoid of promise and this 50,000gns half-sister to five winners returns with her trainer in fine form; can improve.
runners.comment: No threat on sole 2yo start but returns with trainer in fine form; can improve

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper in October on debut; failed to back up that form on next two starts and well beaten at 10-1 over C&D last week on first Flat start.
runners.comment: Form went wrong way in bumpers and well beaten over C&D last week on first Flat start

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Extra Places Handicap

runners.name: Shafi
runners.spotlight: Promise in three 7f novice runs at Southwell, winning on the second occasion; third of the four finishers when upped to 1m for his handicap debut three weeks ago; more will be required for the return to 7f if he is to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Ran okay when third of four finishers on handicap debut last month; needs more back at 7f

runners.name: Lion Of Mali
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose on his handicap debut (7f), after which he was gelded; 2-2 in handicaps this year, winning narrowly at Wolverhampton in February and having more in hand than the head margin indicated over C&D two weeks ago; latest 3lb rise shouldn't prevent another big run.
runners.comment: 2-2 in handicaps this year, value for more than the winning margin over C&D latest; solid

runners.name: Pints In Peace
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; didn't need to improve on earlier Newcastle second to win a 7f maiden at Wolverhampton on last month's stable debut; enjoyed the run of things on that occasion and he will need to pull out more to follow up.
runners.comment: Didn't need to improve to make a winning stable debut last month; this race demands more

runners.name: Ahead Of Fashion
runners.spotlight: 0-8 as a 2yo and ended his juvenile campaign with two below-par efforts; steps up to 7f for his reappearance and he is edging down the weights; the booking of Billy Loughnane is a positive one.
runners.comment: 0-8 as a 2yo and ended 2025 with two disappointing runs; up in trip for seasonal return

runners.name: Black Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the fifth attempt as a 2yo, winning a 6f nursery at Nottingham; two solid efforts over 7f followed; has been gelded; each-way claims back from his 197-day absence.
runners.comment: 6f win at 2yrs; promise over 7f when last seen; has been gelded; each-way shout

runners.name: Deluded
runners.spotlight: The maiden he won at Wolverhampton (7f) last September was a weak one and he found a York sales race too hot to handle one month later; gelded during his absence; looks on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Won a weak Wolverhampton maiden last September; looks on a tough mark for handicap debut

runners.name: Storm Point
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs as a 2yo, notably when keeping on well from a poor position at Wolverhampton in December (6f); gelded soon after; interesting contender now back up to 7f for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promise last year, notably at Wolverhampton (6f); potential big improver in  handicaps

runners.name: Twilight Glow
runners.spotlight: Comfortably the pick of his four 2yo runs came when second of 12 in a 6f maiden a Newbury (good to soft) last September; down the field at Goodwood one month later (Class 4; 7f); gelded since last seen; down in grade for his AW debut but has to prove he is worth this mark.
runners.comment: Newbury 2nd last September a standout effort at 2yrs; needs to prove he's worth this mark

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boosts Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Parisian Scholar
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 on the up with 1m Newcastle victory, and resumed after four months off (gelded) with an excellent second of five to Moment Of Light over C&D 35 days ago; enjoys a 6lb pull in the weights here so not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Excellent second to Moment Of Light over C&D; enjoys a 6lb pull so not taken lightly

runners.name: Moment Of Light
runners.spotlight: Son of Sea The Moon who built on earlier promise after five months off (gelded/tongue tied) when an emphatic winner of handicap over C&D 35 days ago from Parisian Scholar; up 6lb but the sort to go on improving and he rates a big player.
runners.comment: Emphatic winner over C&D latest; up 6lb but sort to go on improving; a big player

runners.name: Starship Trooper
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon colt who resumed from five months off with a solid third of six in 1m4f Southwell novice three weeks ago; he can take a step forward now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Solid third in Southwell novice three weeks ago; can take a step forward now h'capping

runners.name: Green Falcon
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on all his four runs at 2yrs, signing off with a good second of 11 in 1m1f maiden at Yarmouth in September; this son of Kameko needs considering on his first go in handicap company with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Made the frame on all four runs at 2yrs; needs considering on first go in handicap company

runners.name: Devils Peak
runners.spotlight: Free-going juvenile maiden who signed off with a respectable fourth of six in 1m1f nursery at Newmarket in November; this son of Camelot has since been gelded and it would come as no surprise to see him take a step forward on his Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: Free-going juvenile maiden; since gelded and no surprise to see him take a step forward

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Corniche Girl
runners.spotlight: Her only win from 15 attempts came over this C&D and that was her sole appearance at Kempton; not beaten far off this mark in a Wolverhampton handicap (7f) 16 days ago; each-way shout once more.
runners.comment: C&D winner; solid effort off this mark 16 days ago (7f); each-way shout once more
runners.owner: C Munro Mrs M Gander And Partner

runners.name: Uniter
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress from his debut third when trained by Andrew Balding, lacking the pace to land a telling blow over C&D when last seen in January; sold for 10,000gns since; drops in class for his stable debut and Oisin Murphy booked so better could be forthcoming; headgear worn for all three handicap runs now discarded.
runners.comment: Failed to progress for A Balding; drops in class on stable debut; headgear discarded

runners.name: Lovethiswayagain
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in maiden/novice company and hasn't kicked on from her early promise; her mark has dropped 10lb as a result and she makes her handicap debut at a modest level and with Billy Loughnane booked; may now do better.
runners.comment: She hasn't progressed in novice/maiden events but makes handicap debut at a modest level

runners.name: Madman
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 11th attempt when winning a C&D handicap in January; similar form when a close second at Lingfield in February; held by Arry Up on their Leicester run last October.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January; good second at Lingfield in February; should run his race

runners.name: Runamara
runners.spotlight: Regressed after maiden win for previous yard; proved resurgent over 6f at Lingfield (on second run for new stable) most recently and has possibilities provided he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: More encouraging signs at Lingfield in February; now 3lb lower and could go well

runners.name: Chhota Saab
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; gave it a good go from the front at Lingfield in December (5f; blinkered first time) but two disappointing runs have followed; has been gelded since latest run and is down in class with a good apprentice booked so a revival not impossible, for all he carries risk.
runners.comment: 0-7 and well short of his best on last two starts; down in class; gelded since latest run

runners.name: Jungle Knight
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to nurseries last September (7f, turf); gelded prior to his reappearance and now goes in a new hood.
runners.comment: Either the gelding operation or new hood needs to give him a lift judged on his 2yo runs

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: Modest form for Aidan O'Brien; down the field in two 6f handicaps for Tony Carroll (behind Madman over C&D 12 weeks ago) but he has been gelded since the latest and Rossa Ryan now takes over in the saddle.
runners.comment: Down the field in two runs for new yard; gelded since the latest and Rossa Ryan booked

runners.name: Fullstop
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros breeze-up 2yo; showed some promise in three turf maidens last summer; appeals as a likely improver now handicapping after a gelding operation; tongue-tie added for the first time.
runners.comment: Signs of promise in three turf maidens last summer; has been gelded; likely improver

runners.name: Anorah Unleashed
runners.spotlight: Midfield in three runs in fillies' maiden and novice company; no improvement for the switch to nurseries when last seen in October (7f); drops back in trip and into Class 6 company for her return; others still look more persuasive.
runners.comment: Drops back in trip and into her first Class 6 handicap but improvement is essential

runners.name: Arry Up
runners.spotlight: Prominent in the betting when winning a 6f nursery at Leicester (good to soft) when last seen in October; meets runner-up Madman on the same terms and he's an interesting contender with further progress on the cards.
runners.comment: Ready winner of a 6f turf nursery when last seen 176 days ago; remains unexposed

races.name: Virgin Bet Every Saturday Money Back Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: True Promise
runners.spotlight: He has turned in a string of good runs since joining Fionn McSharry; only fifth on his last two starts but dropped 2lb as a result and he has races in him off this sort of mark; Oisin Murphy booked; contender.
runners.comment: Series of solid efforts for his new trainer; on a handy mark and Oisin Murphy booked

runners.name: State Of Madness
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered his form with a bang for Charlie Clover last autumn and he has continued the good work on AW this year, close up in 6f handicaps here and at Southwell on his last two starts; sold for 23,000gns since latest; high on the list starting out for his new yard.
runners.comment: Back to form for C Clover from October onwards; sold 23,000gns in February; key player
runners.owner: The Likely Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Up The Anti
runners.spotlight: Three wins in 2025, the latest a 50-1 surprise over C&D in December; not beaten far here when last seen nine weeks ago; stable may have stronger claims with Serenity Dream.
runners.comment: Popped up at 50-1 over C&D in December; beaten four times since; now upped in class

runners.name: Ruby Red Gove
runners.spotlight: Promise in novice/maiden company and she duly improved to make a winning handicap debut at Wolverhampton last month (6f); better form when a close third at Southwell one week later; feasibly weighted still and should have more to offer after just five starts; Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly who has run two fine races in handicaps; respected on Polytrack debut
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services & Alan Gove Racing

runners.name: Em Four
runners.spotlight: Two wins this year, over 6f at Chelmsford in January and when beating Brazen Idol over C&D two weeks ago (pair prominent throughout); 3lb rise asks more of him but he is likely to give it another good go.
runners.comment: Beat Brazen Idol over C&D two weeks ago, his second win of 2026; 3lb rise to contend with

runners.name: Middleton View
runners.spotlight: He made a winning stable debut in a 5f novice at Chelmsford in December but three handicap runs since have been underwhelming; the addition of a hood needs to give him a lift; stable also runs Expert Agent.
runners.comment: Not kicked on from his novice win in Deceember; now tried in a hood

runners.name: Serenity Dream
runners.spotlight: Ran on well to win a Class 4 over C&D in January; respectable efforts since, including when sixth of seven behind Em Four and Brazen Idol here two weeks ago (held up in a race where the front two dominated); still feasibly weighted.
runners.comment: C&D win in January; race didn't set up for him when 6th to Em Four latest; can bounce back

runners.name: Expert Agent
runners.spotlight: Style of racing can leave him a hostage to fortune but he is well handicapped and has been threatening something bigger of late; should get a pace to aim at this time and he is greatly respected.
runners.comment: Needs a strong pace but he looks in good form and he's on an extremely dangerous mark
runners.owner: J P Morris Mrs V A Tunnicliffe Et Al

runners.name: Brazen Idol
runners.spotlight: Well suited by conditions; made a promising stable debut 14 days ago, forcing Em Four to pull out all the stops, and that was after a 702-day absence; has to back it up but he will be a danger to all if he does.
runners.comment: Good 2nd to Em Four over C&D recently after a 702-day absence; contender if backing it up

races.name: Sunday 12th July Ladies Day At Dundalk Stadium Handicap

runners.name: An Laochmor
runners.spotlight: Belatedly off the mark at 17th attempt when landing odds in Lingfield class 6 handicap in February, coming late after slowish start over this trip; previously second over today's C&D off 43, so more needed but champion jockey takes over.
runners.comment: Lingfield win followed C&D second; more needed off this mark but Keane takes over

runners.name: Star Reign
runners.spotlight: Some promise in 5f turf maidens for Gordon Elliot at the outset of her career; well held on yard/seasonal debut at Naas last month and now tries the AW.
runners.comment: Well held on yard/seasonal debut at Naas last month and now tries the AW

runners.name: Snowballs
runners.spotlight: Glimmers of promise in maidens but has struggled in opening two handicaps; visor tried and Billy Lee takes over from 7lb claimer, so don't rule out an improved showing.
runners.comment: Visor tried and Billy Lee takes over from 7lb claimer; don't rule out an improved showing

runners.name: Cooee Call
runners.spotlight: Three maiden runs here didn't offer much and neither did two handicap runs at the Curragh last summer for Claire O'Connell; cheekpieces replace blinkers on yard debut, but seems best watched after long absence.
runners.comment: Nothing for previous yard; cheekpieces replace blinkers on yard debut but best watched

runners.name: Jazzit
runners.spotlight: Cheap purchase out of Harry Eustace's yard in December and good placed efforts on both starts for new connections here over 6f recently, keen each time and not looking the most straightforward of rides; back in trip and cheekpieces fitted; should go well.
runners.comment: Recent placings over 6f when keen; back in trip and cheekpieces fitted, should go well

runners.name: Sceitimini
runners.spotlight: Recent C&D fifth was encouraging, having not had a clear run; 3yo could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Recent C&D fifth was encouraging, having not had a clear run; 3yo could have more to offer

runners.name: Bye Bye Now
