runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 4yo has progressed in each of four runs here since December, most recently on handicap debut over this trip 12 days ago, making all from wide draw, hanging right towards finish; up 7lb but likely still has more to offer.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner on handicap debut; up 7lb but likely still has more to offer

runners.name: Jordan Breeze
runners.spotlight: In fine form last October under today's rider, winning over C&D off 48 and good second at the Curragh two days later; later same month ran creditably off this mark despite tardy start and absent since; hood now tried on return; draw may not be ideal.
runners.comment: C&D winner in October, bit to do off this mark and draw may not be ideal

runners.name: Shes A Gift
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best effort came on latest over C&D in January when staying-on fourth; 1lb wrong but can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Best effort came on latest over C&D in January when staying on fourth; can't be ruled out

runners.name: Phils Dream
runners.spotlight: Much his best efforts since returning from a lengthy absence in December have come on latest two starts over this trip in Wolverhampton class 6 handicaps, latest when keen enough but asserting inside final furlong off 47; clearly in good heart, likely stiffer task here from a wide draw.
runners.comment: Recent Wolverhampton winner; may need to settle better and wide draw a negative

runners.name: Queen Of Newark
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in Bellewstown 2yo maiden last summer, soft ground may not have suited final start; may need this, although yard among the winners at the weekend.
runners.comment: Reappearing 3yo may need this, although yard among the winners at the weekend

runners.name: Whats It All About
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in a trio of juvenile maidens last year; can only be watched on seasonal debut in first handicap unless market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Nothing in 2yo maidens; can only be watched on seasonal debut in first handicap

races.name: Put The Fun Into Fundraising At Dundalk Handicap

runners.name: Amemri
runners.spotlight: Back to form to record seventh win at the track last month off reduced mark, doing well after being hampered to win well over 7f; 6lb rise okay but only once a winner at this trip, may find this a bit sharp.
runners.comment: Recent 7f winner here; 6lb rise okay but may find this trip a bit sharp

runners.name: Im Spartacus
runners.spotlight: All six wins gained over this trip, most recently fourth win here when up with pace throughout off 62; up 5lb but remains on an okay mark; Caffrey prefers him to Clonmacash.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner up 5lb but remains on an okay mark; Caffrey prefers over Clonmacash

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins over 6f/7f including off 70 in January (7f); generally held his form since, most recently 1.25l fourth to Sporting Hero over C&D, having been slowly away and denied a clear run; 1lb lower and rider now claims 5lb which could bring him right into the mix given better luck in running.
runners.comment: Not much luck in running behind Sporting Hero over C&D latest; rider now claims

runners.name: Edmond Halley
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens and two handicaps on turf (soft/heavy) have been underwhelming; headgear left off and hard to fancy on AW debut.
runners.comment: Two handicaps on turf (soft/heavy) underwhelming; hard to fancy on AW debut

runners.name: Collective Power
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner here, including two of the last three runnings of this race; beaten under 2l behind Sporting Hero over C&D last month and demands respect off 2lb lower mark than when scoring last year.
runners.comment: Twice a winner of this race; demands respect off 2lb lower than when scoring last year
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Mc Loven
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain at 5f (twice on AW); placed at Lingfield over the winter before being bought for 14,000gns in February; dropped out quickly after forcing pace on yard debut in Naas handicap last month on soft; probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: Dropped out quickly after forcing pace on recent yard debut at Naas; best watched for now

runners.name: Inishfallen
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his 6f 2yo win on AW in Britain for Brian Meehan; solid level of form here of late, most recently staying on for second behind all-the-way winner Sporting Hero over this trip last month from a wide draw; 4lb better off with rival here, major chance.
runners.comment: 4lb better off with Sporting Hero on recent C&D second; major chance
runners.owner: F J Carragher/Yorkavenue Syndicate/Park Ave Syndicate

runners.name: Beauty Queen
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f novice races for Kevin Ryan as a juvenile including on AW at Southwell; has become regressive and well beaten in two runs here this year; others more appealing.
runners.comment: Regressive and well beaten in two runs here this year; others more appealing

runners.name: I Bid You Ajou
runners.spotlight: Racked up a good ground hat-trick in a 6f claimer and two 6f handicaps at Fairyhouse early last summer; ground against him final two starts; unexposed on AW and interesting contender, especially if strong in the market on return from winter break.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run but  unexposed on AW; market may prove best guide

runners.name: Rappell
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins in 2024 and last year landed C&D apprentice handicap off 68 in September; absent since midfield behind Collective Power over same trip in November, having been bumped by that rival; lack of a recent run a concern, but jockey booking encouraging.
runners.comment: C&D winner in September; lack of a recent run a concern but jockey booking encouraging

runners.name: Dontspoilasale
runners.spotlight: Well backed when landing a third C&D win in January; 7f too far here last week, return to this trip to suit but more needed.
runners.comment: 7f too far here last week, back to optimum trip but still more needed

runners.name: Sporting Hero
runners.spotlight: Seven of nine career wins have come here, most recently on yard debut over this trip last month when making all off 75 (some rivals inconvenienced by stalls slow to open) to beat Inishfallen 0.75l (Clonmacash fourth, Collective Power sixth); may not get such a soft lead here, but still respected off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Course specialist, making all to beat Inishfallen over C&D last month; respected

races.name: New Saddle Bar At Dundalk Handicap

runners.name: One Cool Bear
runners.spotlight: Maiden went closest here in February when only headed close home over 7f; has run well at this trip too; 4lb rise so more needed.
runners.comment: Just denied over 7f here in February; shorter trip fine, 4lb rise and wide draw not so

runners.name: Cause I Like You
runners.spotlight: Ordinary AW form over the winter, soft ground may have been against her at Naas last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Soft ground may have been against her at Naas last time; still others preferred

runners.name: Stone Bear
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on third handicap attempt over C&D in January off 52; didn't get home over 7f after racing keenly later same month, return to this shorter trip should suit.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January didn't stay 7f last time; return to this shorter trip should suit

runners.name: Faustyna
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear filly was an easy-to-back 14-1 chance when making winning C&D debut in weak maiden in February, keen enough when coming from off pace (fourth won next time) and looking green; opening mark looks workable and has to be respected.
runners.comment: Surprise debut C&D maiden winner in February; opening mark looks workable, respected

runners.name: Lady Landhort
runners.spotlight: Nice bit of improvement to land C&D handicap last month under today's rider, coming from off pace on stands' side, then veering left in beating more experienced rivals; 4lb rise okay and should go well.
runners.comment: Showd inexperience when shock 33-1 C&D winner last month; 4lb rise fine and a contender
runners.owner: T J Flanagan & Ronan Macken & Shane McSweeney & S OMalley

runners.name: Love Bomb
runners.spotlight: Three decent efforts in maidens before getting off the mark on handicap debut at Down Royal (5f, yielding) in September; didn't count on last month's seasonal debut at Naas on soft; 7lb claimer replaced by Billy Lee, so worth a look.
runners.comment: Didn't count on seasonal debut at Naas on soft; 7lb claimer replaced by Billy Lee

runners.name: Royal County Glory
runners.spotlight: Some solid C&D form under today's rider, notably staying-on second and when close fourth in December, having been short of room in straight; one to consider if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Solid C&D form at two; one to consider if strong in the market on return

runners.name: Bullet Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo run when placed in Gowran handicap on heavy (7f), well held on AW debut over a mile in December; best watched on return.
runners.comment: Well held on AW debut over a mile in December; best watched on return
runners.owner: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & D Battersby

runners.name: Jampa Ling
runners.spotlight: Touched off in C&D maiden in February, didn't seem suited by drop to 5f on handicap debut last month against elders; 2lb lower here and return to 6f could see him in the shake-up, despite wide draw.
runners.comment: 5f didn't suit latest; 2lb lower and return to 6f could see him in the shake-up

runners.name: Dream And Believe
runners.spotlight: Reportedly did not handle the surface here in October; caught the eye on last month's seasonal return at Naas (5f, soft), and quite likely he has more to offer if handling surface this time.
runners.comment: Eyecatching seasonal return at Naas; likely more to offer if handling surface
runners.owner: Kildare Racing Synd/Mrs John Murphy/Kilmichael Racing Synd

runners.name: Spirit Above
runners.spotlight: Subject of misplaced gamble in C&D maiden in February, awkward start and plenty green; hood tried (now discarded) at Naas latest but never featured on soft; quite likely capable of better, given more experience, and opening mark allows for that.
runners.comment: Failed to land a touch in C&D maiden in February; may yet do better and opening mark okay

runners.name: Dream Legend
runners.spotlight: Not discredited in C&D handicap in November and possibility of improvement off this 3lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Not discredited in C&D handicap in November and possibility of improvement off 3lb lower
runners.owner: E D Delany & Mrs Lisa Delany

runners.name: Pretty Liz
runners.spotlight: Maiden not beaten far on penultimate 2yo start over C&D; absent since October, however, and watching brief advised.
runners.comment: Absent since October however and watching brief advised

races.name: Dundalk Stadium Open All Summer Maiden

runners.name: Oceans Breath
runners.spotlight: Ran twice over this trip at Navan last October; slow to break when staying on well for 3/4l second behind fellow newcomer Kindergarten on the first occasion (yielding); raced more prominently when odds-on favourite two weeks later, weakened late into fourth, not beaten far; softer ground then might not have suited; good chance this will bring out the best in him.
runners.comment: Showed promise in two Navan starts last October, this surface should suit, leading chance

runners.name: Saint Agatha
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by St Mark's Basilica to five winners for this stable; dam was a 6f Group 3 winner; ran twice over 7f at the Curragh at two; promising debut, close up throughout but ran green and beaten 1/2l; made much of the running before fading from a furlong out in a conditions race won by the talented Diamond Necklace; capable of winning races.
runners.comment: From a family that has done well for the stable, ran twice over 7f at two, can win races
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Allsortz
runners.spotlight: Best of five turf runs last season when fourth in a decent 6f Fairyhouse contest in June; handled this surface when a fair third over C&D in November; official rating of 76 is insufficient to suggest a likely win.
runners.comment: Third over C&D in November, official rating of 76 is insufficient to suggest a likely win

runners.name: Dun Aonghasa
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother by Churchill to two winners; dam a French Listed-placed 2yo winner.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Churchill to two winners, dam a French Listed-placed 2yo winner

runners.name: Green Amoro
runners.spotlight: E140,000 yearling; Dark Angel colt is the first foal out of Que Amore, a Listed sprint winner (five wins in all) who was runner-up to Battaash in the 2020 Nunthorpe; tongue-tie fitted for debut; merits respect.
runners.comment: By Dark Angel, first foal out of a Listed sprint winner and Nunthorpe runner-up, respected

runners.name: Hasita
runners.spotlight: Needs to step up on the form of two juvenile outings at the Curragh to feature here
runners.comment: Needs to step up on the form of two juvenile outings at the Curragh to feature here
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Basilano
runners.spotlight: Failed to cope with heavy ground when completely tailed off on debut at the Curragh; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Massive price when completely tailed off on debut at the Curragh, can be ruled out

runners.name: A Daughters Love
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in four runs at two; no better at the Curragh on return.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in four runs at two, no better at the Curragh on return.

runners.name: Falcon Queen
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Hello Queen, winner of three races for Kevin Ryan, including two on AW; dam a Listed sprint winner for Ryan; favourite on debut in a 7f maiden here in December; raced a bit too keenly and ran out of steam late; hooded now and drops back a furlong; could go close.
runners.comment: Ran out of steam late on debut, hooded now and drops back a furlong, could go close

runners.name: Salama Blue Point
runners.spotlight: E300,000 yearling; solid pedigree; finished towards the back at Naas on only start at two; can do better.
runners.comment: Solid pedigree; finished towards the back at Naas on only start at two, can do better

races.name: Dundalk Stadium Group Deals Available Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Mehmar
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Michael O'Callaghan on turf in 2024, showing a liking for quick ground; placed only once (in a claimer) in seven outings at this venue since last October; soundly beaten at Naas ten days ago
runners.comment: Placed only once (in a claimer) in seven outings at this venue since last October

runners.name: Kitty Bear
runners.spotlight: Opened her account over C&D in January on her eighth AW start; two fair runs since then; finished just in front Alex Belardo and Punk Poet over 7f here 12 days.
runners.comment: Just behind Comfort Line and in front Alex Belardo and Punk Poet over 7f here 12 days ago
runners.owner: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe

runners.name: Alex Belardo
runners.spotlight: Win at Down Royal in 2024 his sole success from 23 starts; however, he handles this surface and ran up a sequence of third placings before finishing fifth here 12 days ago, a run that matches him closely with Comfort Line, Kitty Bear and Punk Poet
runners.comment: Has been showing consistent form at this venue, capable of getting into the money

runners.name: Punk Poet
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner, most effective at this trip; generally below his best since winning here last September; mild encouragement from his most recent run, but needs to reverse form with Comfort Line, Kitty Bear and Punk Poet; dropping in the ratings, now 9lb below last winning mark.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner, generally below his best since winning here last September

runners.name: Deep Vein
runners.spotlight: Not an obvious candidate based on two recent runs here over contrasting trips (7f/1m4f).
runners.comment: Not an obvious candidate based on two recent runs here over contrasting trips (7f/1m4f)

runners.name: Hi George
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in five runs before a lengthy break; slowly away and never involved in a 6f handicap here on return.
runners.comment: Poor overall form, never involved in a 6f handicap here on return from a long break

runners.name: Master Garvey
runners.spotlight: Has done a lot of racing since a C&D win just over a year ago, one of only two wins in a 46-race career; two fair runs over a longer trip here this year; lack of stamina looked like an issue, return to this distance should help.
runners.comment: C&D winner with modest strike rate, fair recent form over further, return to 1m will suit
runners.owner: Patrick M OBrien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins

runners.name: Star Mind
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner in Britain; best run at this venue so far when dead-heating for third with Palazzo Persico in a claimer last Friday; this looks a stronger race.
runners.comment: Dead-heating for third with Palazzo Persico in a claimer last week, more on his plate now

runners.name: Palazzo Persico
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner for Ed Dunlop (three on AW); won four-runner 1m2f Brighton handicap last July; best run for this yard so far when a dead-heating third of nine with Star Mind over the extended 1m2f here last Friday; this trip is possibly a bit shorter than ideal.
runners.comment: Shared third with Star Mind last Friday, this trip is possibly a bit shorter than ideal

runners.name: Alto Sax
runners.spotlight: Two wins on turf in 2024; 0-11 on AW; has failed to reach the first six in five visits since last October; jockey booking the only positive.
runners.comment: 2-14 turf, 0-11 AW, has failed to reach the first six in five visits since last October

runners.name: Comfort Line
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner with his last win coming over C&D this time last year off 72; now down to 59 and comes into the reckoning on his good run here on his penultimate start, with several of these rivals behind; however, he failed to reproduce that form here last Friday.
runners.comment: No impact here last Friday, had several of these rivals when runner-up here a week earlier

races.name: BAR 1 Betting "Great App, No Joke" Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Emmeleia
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly; half-sister to 2025 Lingfield Derby Trial winner; won a 7f barrier trial at Leopardstown last August, beating Breaking Dawn, a Curragh maiden winner this season; confirmed promise when second in a 7f maiden here in September; winner Yellowstone Lake had previously finished a close-up fourth behind the high-class Diamond Necklace at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Won a barrier trial, second in a 7f course maiden last September, the form looks solid

runners.name: Chicago Moon
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a Leopardstown barrier trial last August, improved from an initial C&D outing to finish third behind two colts here a month ago; this is a fillies-only event but it could be a stronger maiden.
runners.comment: Third behind two colts here a month ago but this fillies' race could be stronger
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Believed
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Frankel to four winners, notably 2022 Melbourne Cup hero Gold Trip; unplaced dam (by Dubai Destination), half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Sarkiyla; should have no trouble with this trip on debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Frankel to four winners notably Melbourne Cup hero Gold Trip

runners.name: Ipanema Sunshine
runners.spotlight: E12,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to winners including Listed scorer Blue Sky Dreamer; dam placed, out of a sister to 6f Group 2 Richmond Stakes winner Revenue.
runners.comment: E12,000 yearling, half-sister to winners including Listed scorer Blue Sky Dreamer

runners.name: Eretria
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to three winners; dam 6f 2yo winner, out of half-sister to Irish 2,000 Guineas and Irish Derby winner Desert King; stablemate Emmeleia has the advantage of experience.
runners.comment: Dam a well-related 6f winner, stablemate Emmeleia has the advantage of experience.

runners.name: Zitkala Sa
runners.spotlight: 26,000gns yearling; 12th foal; closely related to US turf winner Legend Emma, half-sister to six winners; dam 1m4f winner, half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Tuscan Evening.
runners.comment: Closely related to US turf winner Legend Emma, half-sister to six winners, dam 1m4f winner

runners.name: Music And Song
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in two outings over shorter trips at this venue; hooded now; safe to ignore.
runners.comment: Has struggled in two outings over shorter trips at this venue, hooded now, safe to ignore

runners.name: Yousaynothingatall
runners.spotlight: Sister to Wemighttakedlongway, a 1m2f Group 3 winner last year and second to Minnie Hauk in the Irish Oak; ran twice last autumn; improved from Cork debut when fourth at Gowran; can do better again with the trip likely to suit;
runners.comment: Ran twice last autumn, improved from Cork run when fourth at Gowran, can do better again

runners.name: Cosmetic
runners.spotlight: 14th foal; closely related to Group 1 winners Hydrangea, The United States and Hermosa; last of nine behind a stablemate on debut, hampered at an early stage and reported coughing post-race; no excuse other than a sluggish start when she failed to improve at Galway on her second start; ground was testing for both starts, this surface may prove suitable.
runners.comment: Superb pedigree, ground was testing for both 2yo starts, this surface may prove suitable
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

races.name: View Restaurant Summer Offer Handicap

runners.name: Sarmiento Power
runners.spotlight: C&D win in September off 13lb lower; some good efforts since although didn't appear to stay 2m latest; contender back in trip here.
runners.comment: C&D winner didn't appear to stay 2m latest; contender back in trip

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Goodwood novice last year but safely held afterwards in handicaps for James Horton; bought for 5,000gns in December and probably best watched on Irish debut.
runners.comment: Good wood novice winner bought for 5,000gns in December and best watched on Irish debut

runners.name: Beat The Devil
runners.spotlight: Easy winner over 1m here in January and best effort since on latest when staying-on second to Baila Conmigo over 1m2f last month; beaten favourite only previous try at this longer trip; Keane takes over.
runners.comment: Staying on second to Baila Conmigo over 1m2f last month; unproven over this far

runners.name: Breathe Again
runners.spotlight: Now a 15-race maiden, absent since poor Curragh run in October; only second AW start and hard to have confidence in, despite reduced mark.
runners.comment: Only second AW start and hard to have confidence in, despite reduced mark

runners.name: Yosemite Gold
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner last year has run well enough in four Flat starts including on AW when trained in Britain; fair bit to find with Sir Callisto on recent C&D run, although that was seasonal debut so could improve; hood now re-fitted.
runners.comment: Fair bit to find with Sir Callisto on recent C&D run; hood now re-fitted

runners.name: Echinacea
runners.spotlight: Maiden has finished runner-up on three of five runs since joining this yard, most recently over 2m last month in amateur handicap when made plenty of use of; equally effective at this trip, one to consider with professional back aboard.
runners.comment: Second in 2m amateur handicap here recently; one to consider with professional back on

runners.name: Baila Conmigo
runners.spotlight: Progressing well for current yard, recording second win here in last three starts when beating staying-on Beat The Devil last month (1m2f); equally effective at this longer trip and good chance of confirming that form off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Fine form here of late, good chance of confirming latest win over Beat The Devil

runners.name: Edge Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on turf for Charlie Johnston; recent form here leaves him with plenty to find, although has plummeted in the weights.
runners.comment: Plenty to find on recent efforts, although has plummeted in the weights

runners.name: Expound
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose 7yo whose Flat wins have been on turf; 0-11 on AW though and this trip could stretch him.
runners.comment: Best form over shorter; 0-11 on AW and this trip could stretch him

runners.name: Billie Frechette
runners.spotlight: Had a good year last term, winning at Down Royal and should have won the 2m2f Ulster Cesarewitch (denied clear run); just fair sixth over today's C&D when last seen in October; market probably best guide on return.
runners.comment: Good year last season, market probably best guide on return

runners.name: Sir Callisto
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last year off 2lb higher and some decent turf efforts in defeat after; last month's C&D comeback third was encouraging and one for the shortlist now.
runners.comment: Last month's C&D comeback third was encouraging and one for the shortlist now

runners.name: Brandon Creek
runners.spotlight: Off the mark here over 1m2f in November off 58; didn't land a blow over today's trip when last seen in February and stamina to prove now.
runners.comment: Didn't land a blow over today's trip latest; stamina remains unproven

runners.name: Derry Lad
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner at up to 1m4f; back with former yard after a fruitless spell for Emmet Mullins when looking not to stay 2m here early last month; back to a more suitable trip.
runners.comment: Didn't appear to stay 2m here early last month; back to a more suitable trip

races.name: Shek Kip Mei Handicap (Class 5) (Dirt)

runners.name: Viva Chaleur
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame several times over 6f-1m1f on the dirt here, but is now 0-35 in Hong Kong; others stronger.
runners.comment: Has made the frame several times on the dirt here, but is now 0-35 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Tai Victory
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but no closer than eighth in 11 starts in Hong Kong including three over C&D; hard to recommend despite the drop in grade.
runners.comment: No closer than eighth in 11 starts in Hong Kong; hard to recommend despite drop in grade

runners.name: Noble Deluxe
runners.spotlight: Turf efforts this year have been poor, but he did win over C&D in January; still 7lb higher, but being proven under the conditions counts for plenty and he has the inside stall.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in January; 7b higher but being proven under the conditions counts for plenty

runners.name: Taihang Scenery
runners.spotlight: 0-14; fourth over C&D last July and ran well on his return when runner-up on turf at Happy Valley (5f) in February, but didn't back it up there (6f) a fortnight later; hood returns; not sure what to expect.
runners.comment: 0-14; has run well over C&D and made a promising reappearance, but well held next time

runners.name: Racingrace
runners.spotlight: 0-11 overall, and well beaten in two previous goes over C&D; will need to improve plenty for the addition of blinkers.
runners.comment: 0-11 overall, and well beaten in two previous goes over C&D; blinkers on
runners.owner: D Mong Tak Yeung J Mong Shuk Mei & J Mong Shuk Ching

runners.name: Island Breezes
runners.spotlight: Beaten only a nose on turf at Happy Valley (5f) in December, but has regressed since and now 0-16; drawn widest and hard to know how he will cope with dirt.
runners.comment: 0-16; drawn widest and hard to know how he will cope with dirt

runners.name: Triumphant Warrior
runners.spotlight: 0-24 and drawn wider than ideal, but has been placed in three of his six starts on dirt here, including two over this trip; could sneak into the frame.
runners.comment: 0-24 but placed in three of his six starts on dirt here; could sneak into the frame
runners.owner: Bernard Man Stephen Man Shiu Fai & Gerard Man

runners.name: Cheer For South
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D a year ago so dirt is no problem, but that remains his only success in 31 starts; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Won over C&D a year ago so dirt is no problem, but that is his only success in 31 starts
runners.owner: Chan Man Chun James Mathew Fong Et Al

runners.name: Legendary Impact
runners.spotlight: Reserve; out of the frame in 13 starts on turf in Hong Kong; drops in class and cheekpieces return, but more will be needed now switched to dirt if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in 13 starts on turf in Hong Kong; drops in class but more will be needed

runners.name: Superb Guy
runners.spotlight: 0-11; twice placed on turf over 7f/1m, but well beaten in two starts over 1m on dirt here; drop to Class 5 is a positive, but he's drawn out wide and makes limited appeal.
runners.comment: 0-11; drop to Class 5 is a positive, but he's drawn wide and makes limited appeal

runners.name: Macanese Master
runners.spotlight: In good form since December including two wins on turf and finishing third behind Noble Deluxe and Only U over C&D in January; has a bit to find with that pair on these revised terms, though.
runners.comment: Third behind Noble Deluxe and Only U here in January but badly in with them on these terms

runners.name: Only U
runners.spotlight: Just 1-32 in Hong Kong, but running well in defeat lately including when runner-up to Noble Deluxe over C&D in January; 6lb better off and well drawn; leading player.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat lately including when runner-up over C&D in January; leading player

runners.name: Double Bingo
runners.spotlight: Makes stable debut after four months off and limited encouragement in his barrier trials, but he was placed in four consecutive starts over C&D between May and October last year; no headgear today, but still interesting with his rider taking off 10lb.
runners.comment: Placed in four consecutive starts over C&D between May and October last year; interesting
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Glory Cloud
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 1-24, but the win came over C&D in December which was the last time he contested a Class 5; worth a second look if he gets a run.
runners.comment: 1-24, but the win came over C&D in December which was the last time he contested a Class 5

races.name: Yue Wan Handicap (Class 5) (Dirt)

runners.name: Wah May Wai Wai
runners.spotlight: Reserve; handy sort who is 0-25 on turf in Hong Kong despite plummeting in the weights; the switch to dirt needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: 0-25 on turf in Hong Kong; switch to dirt needs to make a big difference

runners.name: Jolly Jumper
runners.spotlight: 1-21 and will need to fare better than in his two previous attempts on dirt here if he is to figure; can make the running.
runners.comment: Will need to fare better than in his two previous attempts on dirt if he is to figure
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate (T1)

runners.name: Meepmeep
runners.spotlight: Just 1-29, though he has won once and made the frame four times from eight starts over C&D; had dropped a long way in the weights prior to finishing runner-up to Go Go Go back here 17 days ago and is now 6lb better off; could go well.
runners.comment: 1-29, though he has won once and made the frame four times from eight starts over C&D

runners.name: Phantom Cyclone
runners.spotlight: 0-21 in Hong Kong, but has made the frame in four of his six starts over C&D including when fourth (behind three of these) 17 days ago; frame possibilities again.
runners.comment: 0-21 in Hong Kong, but has made the frame in four of his six starts over C&D

runners.name: Conspiracy Witness
runners.spotlight: 0-14; didn't run badly in one previous start over C&D, but still needs to show improvement for the addition of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-14; needs to show improvement for the addition of cheekpieces
runners.owner: Arthur Antonio Da Silva Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva

runners.name: All Eyes On Me
runners.spotlight: 0-16, but achieved his first placing when third behind Go Go Go and Meepmeep over C&D 17 days ago; claims if taking another step forward.
runners.comment: 0-16, but achieved his first placing when third over C&D 17 days ago; each-way claims
runners.owner: Enrico Chow Yue Hin & Jennifer Ng Mau Yee

runners.name: Sparkle And Gold
runners.spotlight: Reserve; unplaced in 11 starts on turf; switch to dirt and longer trip leave him with enough to prove.
runners.comment: Unplaced in 11 starts; switch to dirt and longer trip leave him with enough to prove

runners.name: Go Go Go
runners.spotlight: Took his record to 2-38 when making all to beat Meepmeep over C&D days ago; 7lb rise and wide draw will make a follow up that much tougher.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D 17 days ago; now 7lb higher and is drawn wide

runners.name: Diriya
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in eight starts on turf, but drops in grade and Zac Purton takes the ride for this dirt debut; respected from the inside stall.
runners.comment: 0-8, but drops in grade and Zac Purton takes the ride for this dirt debut

runners.name: Hailtothevictors
runners.spotlight: 0-12, but now 5lb lower then when runner-up from a high draw over C&D in December; better berthed this time and could go well.
runners.comment: 0-12, but now 5lb lower then when runner-up from a high draw over C&D in December
runners.owner: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau

runners.name: Happydearhappydeer
runners.spotlight: 0-11 and hasn't shown a great deal in three starts since returning from over a year off in January, but has been placed in three of his five outings on dirt here (1m/1m1f); not dismissed now back over a more suitable trip.
runners.comment: 0-11, but placed in three of his five outings on dirt here; not dismissed
runners.owner: Hong Yiu Lynn Fu Lam Wu Et Al

runners.name: Verbier
runners.spotlight: 2-26 with both wins coming on turf at Happy Valley; out of the frame in four goes on dirt, though, so has enough to prove.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner at Happy Valley, but out of the frame in four goes on dirt

runners.name: Celestial Harmony
runners.spotlight: 1-18 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D in January; held off this 6lb higher mark back here last month, but is better drawn than he was then; each-way claims.
runners.comment: C&D winner; better drawn than he was last time; each-way claims

runners.name: Telecom Power
runners.spotlight: 1-30 on turf in Hong Kong; on a fair mark, but the switch to dirt and a high draw pose questions.
runners.comment: 1-30 on turf in Hong Kong; switch to dirt and a high draw pose questions
runners.owner: Cheung King Shek Billy Cheung King Shan Et Al

runners.name: On The Lash
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf here (1m1f) in the autumn, but out of the frame in three goes on dirt and has the outside stall.
runners.comment: Won twice on turf here in the autumn, but well held in three goes on dirt; drawn widest

runners.name: Spangle Fortune
runners.spotlight: 2-34 in Hong Kong with the wins coming over 6f/1m on dirt here, but not seen since virtually pulled up over C&D six months ago; runner-up in an AW barrier trial last month, but opposable from his draw.
runners.comment: Dual dirt winner here, but not seen in a race in six months; drawn wide

races.name: Nam Shan Handicap (Class 4) (Dirt)

runners.name: Never Peter Out
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over C&D and he's now 9lb lower than for the latest after several indifferent efforts, but he was only beaten just over a length into fifth (behind two of these) here 17 days ago; has the inside draw and Zac Purton rides him for the first time; respected.
runners.comment: 9lb lower than for the last of his four C&D wins; not beaten far last time; respected

runners.name: Leslie
runners.spotlight: Dropping back down the weights and now just 2lb higher than when winning from a wide draw over C&D in October; frame possibilities.
runners.comment: 2lb higher than when winning from a wide draw over C&D in October; frame possibilities

runners.name: Daily Trophy
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over 6f on dirt here and he's now 1lb lower than when last successful, but his form beyond that trip raises significant concerns.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than for the last of four wins over 6f on dirt here, but modest beyond that trip
runners.owner: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho

runners.name: Cant Go Wong
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 5lb lower than when beaten a nose on turf at Happy Valley (1m) in November, but not built on it and is now just 2-49 in Hong Kong; hasn't shown much aptitude for dirt in five previous attempts.
runners.comment: Just 2-49 in Hong Kong; hasn't shown much aptitude for dirt in five previous attempts

runners.name: Bull Attitude
runners.spotlight: 1-16; has run well to finish fourth over C&D in his last two starts (splitting two of these last time), but he hasn't been done many favours by the draw.
runners.comment: Fourth over C&D in his last two starts, but he hasn't been done many favours by the draw

runners.name: Fashion Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner last year and 2lb lower than for the latest in June, but he isn't proving very consistent and has the outside stall.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner last year and 2lb lower than for the latest, but isn't very consistent
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Night Purosangue
runners.spotlight: 1-16 with the win coming in a Class 5 over C&D in October; only beaten a length into third from a high draw here 17 days ago with Bull Attitude and Never Peter Out not far behind, but is again drawn wide.
runners.comment: C&D winner and finished third from a high stall here last time, but is again drawn wide

runners.name: Good Luck Babe
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner here (7f/1m) and just beaten over 6f on dirt last September; 4lb below his last winning mark and has the form to make an impact at this level if on a going day.
runners.comment: 4lb below his last winning mark and has the form to make an impact if on a going day

runners.name: Nova Knight

runners.owner: Gan Wee Sean Gary Gan Cheng Chuah & Gavin Gan Cheng Hooi

runners.name: Kyrus Treasure

runners.comment: 0-5 but has shown some ability; switches to dirt and remains unexposed

runners.name: Supreme Agility
runners.spotlight: 1-25 in Hong Kong, the win coming narrowly over C&D in November; held off this mark on turf 11 days later and not seen in a race since; placed in two of his three recent barrier trials, but Zac Purton jumps ship; best watched.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but not seen in a race since November and Zac Purton jumps ship; best watched

races.name: Ma Tau Wai Handicap (Class 4) (Dirt)

runners.name: Swagger Bro
runners.spotlight: 0-14 in Hong Kong, but has run with credit in all three starts over C&D and is much lower in the weights now; could make an impact.
runners.comment: Has run with credit in all three starts over C&D and is much lower in the weights now

runners.name: Star Performer
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 1-7 in South Africa and unplaced in 12 starts on turf in Hong Kong; has to prove himself on dirt and does look better at 5f
runners.comment: Unplaced in 12 starts in Hong Kong; has to prove himself on dirt and looks better at 5f

runners.name: No Other Choice
runners.spotlight: 0-11, but has run a couple of blinders from the widest stall over C&D within the past six months, going down by a short head in October and finishing a close third last time; has the inside draw today; player.
runners.comment: Has run two fine races from the widest stall here in the past six months; rails draw today
runners.owner: Cheng Chung Wah & Enzo Cheng Tin Hang

runners.name: Fortune Whiskey
runners.spotlight: Could be given a chance on his near miss off 5lb higher over C&D in October, but consistency isn't proving his strong suit.
runners.comment: Could be given a chance on his best form, but consistency isn't proving his strong suit
runners.owner: Alan Tsang Ping Sing Fiona Tsang Wai Sze Et Al

runners.name: Rising Elite
runners.spotlight: Well held in four starts over 6f/1m on dirt/turf here; enough to prove with cheekpieces added to the existing headgear.
runners.comment: Well held in four starts on dirt/turf here; enough to prove with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Lucky Field
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hard to get excited about his six efforts over 6f/1m on turf; needs a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Hard to get excited about his six efforts on turf; needs a drop in grade
runners.owner: Cheng Yu Wai Cheng Mei Mei Et Al

runners.name: Day Day Victory
runners.spotlight: First three wins came over 5f on turf at Happy Valley, but the latest was over this C&D in November; held in four starts since, but is edging back down the weights and no surprise were he to run well.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in November; edging back down the weights and could run well
runners.owner: Alan Kiang Ping Fai & Joyce Lee

runners.name: Flowing Riches
runners.spotlight: Sole win in nine starts came on turf here (5f) last September, but he finished third over C&D in his last two outings (first-time blinkers on latest occasion); should go well again.
runners.comment: Has finished third over C&D in his last two outings; should go well again

runners.name: Starry Show
runners.spotlight: 1-3 in Australia, but little impact in seven local starts over 5f-7f on turf; switch to dirt needs to make a big difference and he doesn't have the best of draws.
runners.comment: Little impact in seven local starts on turf; switch to dirt needs to make a big difference

runners.name: Tactical Victory
runners.spotlight: Has beaten a total of three rivals in six starts on turf in Hong Kong and the drop to Class 4 made no difference last time; one glimmer of hope is that one of his three wins in Australia came on a synthetic surface.
runners.comment: Moderate in six starts on turf in Hong Kong, but won on a synthetic surface in Australia

runners.name: Pi Legend
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all five local starts, but has shown ability as when fifth over C&D last month; the drop in class and his rider's 10lb claim are positives, but his draw isn't.
runners.comment: Has shown ability; drop in class and rider's 10lb claim are positives, but his draw isn't

runners.name: Lucky Together
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia, but well held in five local starts over 6f/7f on turf; down in class, but has an awkward draw for this dirt debut.
runners.comment: 0-5 in Hong Kong; down in class, but has an awkward draw for this dirt debut

runners.name: King Oberon
runners.spotlight: Three wins over this trip on turf last year, but a rise in the weights seems to have found him out in his last three starts; needs to find extra for the switch to dirt.
runners.comment: A rise in the weights seems to have found him out in his last three starts; dirt debut

runners.name: Speedy Smartie

runners.comment: Record since last June reads 2121214212; up another 2lb but still hard to oppose
runners.owner: Dr Andrew Yu Dr Alvin Yu Man Ho & Dr Bernice Yu Man Yan

races.name: Ping Shek Handicap (Class 4) (Dirt)

runners.name: Mega Force
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all 15 Hong Kong starts; some of his more encouraging runs were on this surface but he's eligible for lower-grade races now and is difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Unplaced all 15 Hong Kong starts and difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Northern Beast
runners.spotlight: Placed on turf at Happy Valley (1m) in February, after a break, but finished well down the field on next two outings and can't be supported with much confidence on this rare dirt start.
runners.comment: Well down the field on last two turf starts; makes limited appeal on rare dirt run
runners.owner: Ngai Man Amy Wang Hong & Doris Ngai Yan Tung

runners.name: Firefoot
runners.spotlight: Just 1-23 on turf but launched dirt career with two creditable thirds this year (1m1f/1m) and didn't run badly when midfield here on latest outing; a possible.
runners.comment: Has taken well to dirt this year (placed twice); a possible

runners.name: Yodas Choice
runners.spotlight: Dual dirt winner in 2025 (1m1f/1m); didn't fire last time but was placed on turf (7f) two starts ago and has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Dual dirt winner in 2025 (1m1f/1m); didnt fire last time but still considered each-way
runners.owner: Alan Lee Yu Sing & Exors Of The Estate Of The Late Lee Kwok

runners.name: Sure Joyful

runners.comment: Well below best on turf this season and unraced on dirt since 2023

runners.comment: Dead-heated in Class 5 turf event last month; needs to kick on again on this dirt debut

runners.name: Ariel
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; won three times on dirt last year (1m-1m1f) but has been out of form lately and has tough draw today.
runners.comment: Triple dirt winner in 2025 but out of form lately and has tough draw today

runners.name: Special Hedge

runners.comment: Won on turf in October but needs to up his game after some substandard efforts

runners.name: Lucky Year
runners.spotlight: Reserve; unexposed 4yo who showed clear promise on all three turf starts (7f-1m); not out to this if suited by the new trip/surface.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who showed clear promise on all three turf starts (7f-1m); dirt debut

runners.name: Ocean Impact
runners.spotlight: Scored in first-time blinkers over this trip on turf in November and has been very competitive off new mark this year; below form on latest outing but still enters calculations on dirt debut.
runners.comment: Below form on latest outing but still enters calculations on dirt debut

runners.name: Shanghai Style
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent this season but ran okay on turf last month and is sure to be suited by this return to dirt (dual course winner); can be very dangerous off current mark if in the right mood.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this season but dirt suits him well and he's on a good mark; interesting

runners.comment: Outran big odds on dirt debut in February but well held since; cheekpieces now added
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Philip Ng Yuk Wing & Michael Ng Mun Kit

runners.comment: Consistent 6yo who has taken well to dirt lately; probably won't be far away

runners.spotlight: In-form 5yo who won readily on this surface here (1m) in February and stayed on to go close on his next outing; seems likely to be suited by the extra furlong today; must be considered.
runners.comment: In good form over 1m on dirt in recent weeks, and this extra 1f seems likely to suit

runners.name: The Lion King
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on his last two turf starts (1m/7f) and is evidently in good nick, but outside draw could make things awkward on dirt debut.
runners.comment: In good form on turf lately but outside draw makes things awkward on dirt debut

runners.name: Glorious Success

runners.name: Justifying

runners.comment: Dropped 2lb since last month's C&D fifth; each-way claims if aided by headgear switch

runners.comment: Good second from stall 12 over C&D last month; much better drawn today; one to consider
runners.owner: Tam Man Wai Wilson Tam Man Shu & Dr Tam Po Chor

runners.name: Good Chap

runners.comment: Gave good account on turf last month but remains on fairly tough mark

runners.name: Notthesillyone
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D towards end of last year and was also on target over 5f on turf here a fortnight ago; well drawn for this dirt return and could plausibly still be on the upgrade; respected.

runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on his first three Hong Kong starts; dirt debut today
runners.owner: Wong Kam Choi & Benson Wang Ding Ben

runners.name: Fun N Fun Together
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on early-season form but managed only eighth over C&D when dipping back into this grade last month and Zac Purton has jumped ship today.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on early-season form but only eighth when back in this grade last month

runners.name: Wolf Coming

runners.comment: Placed on turf this year; drops in grade with Zac Purton enlisted on dirt debut

runners.name: Blasted Talent
runners.spotlight: Made low-key debut when ninth on turf here (7f) in December; needs this new surface to show him in a much better light.
runners.comment: Made low-key debut when ninth on turf here (7f) in December

runners.name: Capital Delight
runners.spotlight: Gained his last four wins over C&D but has looked out of sorts this year, even when back over this C&D last month; revival needed.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner; out of sorts this year and needs a revival

runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season and still in very good form; likely contender

runners.name: Packing Fighter

runners.comment: 0-5 in HK but unlucky not to finish closer than fifth here recently; dirt debut today

runners.name: Noisy Boy

runners.comment: Regressive 5yo who is yet to be placed after eight starts this season; others preferred

runners.name: Telecom Fighters

runners.comment: Back up in grade after recent turf win but this surface also suits him

runners.comment: C&D winner on last month's dirt debut; back up in grade and drawn wide today, though

runners.name: So You Will
runners.spotlight: 0-4 in Hong Kong but kept on well to make the frame over 1m on his last two turf starts; contender if suited by this switch to dirt.
runners.comment: 0-4 in Hong Kong but kept on well to make the frame over 1m on his last two turf starts

runners.name: Joltin

runners.comment: Has not shown a great deal on turf in HK (three runs); drawn wide for dirt debut

runners.name: Sturdy Ruby

runners.comment: Placed over C&D in January but will be 5lb wrong if he gets a run today

runners.name: Romantic Thor
